Execute Commands Relative To Blocks (@b) [14w21b] Redstone Device Map Here are a series of commands that will allow you to /execute relative to a specific block. (Tested in 14w21b) Remember to leave a Diamond to show your support! ______________________________________________________________________________ /scoreboard objectives add BlockExecute dummy /fill ~7 ~-1 ~ ~ ~-1 ~ redstone_block /fill ~7 ~1 ~ ~ ~1 ~ /execute @a ~-2 ~-2 ~-2 /fill ~ ~ ~ ~4 ~4 ~4 double_plant 0 replace /execute @a ~-4 ~-4 ~-4 /fill ~ ~ ~ ~8 ~8 ~8 double_plant 0 replace /scoreboard players set @e[type=Item] BlockExecute 1 {Age:0s,Item:{id:minecraft:double_plant}} /execute @e[score_BlockExecute=1,score_BlockExecute_min=1] ~ ~ ~ (optional) /execute @e[score_BlockExecute=1,score_BlockExecute_min=1] ~ ~ ~ /setblock ~ ~ ~ /kill @e[score_BlockExecute=1,score_BlockExecute_min=1] /scoreboard players reset * BlockExecute ______________________________________________________________________________ EXPLAINATION: Relative to the player, a /fill replace command is executed. This replaces all of the blocks of the player's choosing with a "double_plant" block. When updated, the double_plant block will break into an item and from this entity we execute our commands. After the commands have been executed the double_plant item is destroyed and the scoreboard is reset. Special thanks to dandamannnnn for his Destroy Specific Blocks command set up. ______________________________________________________________________________ Redstone, Block, Command, Tutorial, Redstone Device, Commands, Clock, Relative

The Command Box Redstone Device Map English:The Command Box is a giant Bedrock box that let your game a bit "different". This will make your game:* Be always in hard mode* You can't craft a Wooden Sword* You can't craft a Golden Sword* Your health doesn't regenerate naturaly, only with potions, etc.Note: When you craft one Wooden/Golden Sword, it will broke and you will receive the resources back (You will receive a stick and two Oak Wood Planks if you craft a Wooden Sword and will receive a stick and two gold ingots if you craft a Golden Sword)And there are some skills:* Resistance: when you take damage 150 times, you will be rewarded with Resistance II for the rest of your life!* Strenght: When you deal damage 150 times, you will be rewarded with Strenght for the rest of your life!* Water Breathing: When you swin 60000 cm, you will be rewarded with Water Breathing for the rest of your life!Note: When you die, the skills will continue with you. Example: If you has Resistance II by the skill, if you die 2, 3 times you will ever has the skill!And one more thing: DON'T KILL WOLVES! When you kill seven wolves, you will be punished with Weakness 256 for the rest of your life! This means that you will be unable to hit anyone and any entity with any item. So, you with a Diamond Sword punching a Chicken will not do anything on her. Anything will be able to kill you and you will can't do nothing! To be pardoned by the wolves you will need to be killed by three other wolves!Português:A Command Box é uma caixa de Bedrock (Matriz) gigante, que irá alterar o seu jogo no Survival. Isso fara com que:* O seu mundo sempre esteja no Hard Mode* Você não consiga craftar uma Espada de Madeira* Você não consiga craftar uma Espada de Ouro* Seu HP (corações) não regenerem naturalmente, apenas com poções, etc.Obs.: Quando você crafta uma Espada de Madeira ela irá quebrar instantâneamente e você receberá 2 Oak Wood Planks e 1 stick, já com a Espada de Ouro, você receberá 2 Gold Ingots e 1 stick.E também há algumas habilidades:* Resistência: Se você tomar 150 hits no mesmo mapa, você será recompensado com Resistance II!* Força: Se você der 150 hits em qualquer mob ou player no mesmo mapa, você será recompensado com Strenght!* Respiração: Se você nadar ao todo 60000 cm, você será recompensado com Water Breathing!Obs: Se você morrer você não perde nenhuma das habilidades acima!E mais uma coisa: NÃO MATE LOBOS! Se você matar 7 lobos você será castigado com Weakness 256. Assim, você não poderá bater em nada (exemplo: você mesmo com uma Espada de Diamante batendo em uma galinha, ela não tomará dano algum) e isso só pode ser revertido se o player for assassinado por 3 lobos.Let your ideas in the comments! I will check them and depending I could add this to The Command Box. This helps so much!Deixe suas ideias nos comentários! Eu as lerei e talvez eu possa colocá-las na Command Box. Isso ajuda muito! Semi-automatic Water Bucket Filling/Emptying Station Redstone Device Map Are you a lazy person who needs water buckets to be filled? Or do you need tons of water buckets to be filled. It would take a huge amount of time just to fill up those water buckets. You could be doing something else in that time. Well, for those people, you are in luck. I have created a compact version of a Semi-automatic Water Filling Station. All you need to do is to put ya water buckets in the chest and the machine does the rest! Just remember to pick up the buckets after they are done. This machine is 5 by 8 by 5. The resources you need to build this are:-10 Hoppers-15 Redstone Dust-7 Repeaters-37 Blocks-2 Comparators-5 Redstone Torches-4 Droppers-1 Dispenser-4 Slabs-2 Trapped Chests-At least 2 Regular Chest-2 Water Buckets/ Water Sources-2 Noteblocks (Optional)This machine is compatible with 1.7+.There is a schematic and a world download for you to check out iif you are interested! [Gamemode] Master Hardcore Mode V1 Redstone Device Map Master Hardcore Mode is a Gamemode-in-a-box that changes the rules for how Minecraft is played.Just add it to a world's spawn chunk (I'll post a world soon from my Let's Play).The three new rules are:It's always night after the first bit of day (you have an option to play a full day).Animals don't give you food (so farming is important).You can't craft chests (though Ender chests and naturally spawned chests are ok to use).The box enforces all these rules for you and also turns Ultra Hardcore on by default (you have an option on the back to turn that off too).Requires the newer 1.8 snapshots (for the updated clear command). Enjoy! Redstone, Redstone Device, Gamemode

Player Launch Pad in Vanilla Minecraft Redstone Device Map I have created a Player Launch Pad in vanilla minecraft. When the players steps onto the launch pad, they will be launched into the air! The world comes with an already set up launch pad course to demonstrate how it works.By editing the number in the command blocks under the individual launch pads you can adjust the power of the launch pads. BattleShip Bismark -- Nazi Warship Water Structure Map This Is A Schematic Of The Nazi Warship Bismark Who Sailed During WW2.The Story Of The Bismark's Last BattleThe Battle of the Denmark Strait, also known as the Iceland Battle, was a brief naval engagement of little more than a quarter of an hour. It was a clash of titans in which the largest warships in the world were put to the test, and it will be remembered as a battle that ended in the sinking of a mythic ship.In the early morning of 24 May, the weather improved and the visibility increased. The German battle group maintained a course of 220º and a speed of 28 knots, when at 0525, the Prinz Eugen's hydrophones detected propeller noises of two ships on her port side. At 0537 the Germans sighted what they first thought to be a light cruiser at about 19 miles (35,190 meters / 38,480 yards) on port side. At 0543, another unidentified unit was sighted to port, and thereafter the alarm was given aboard the Bismarck and Prinz Eugen. Aboard the Bismarck the identification of the enemy ships was uncertain, and they were now both mistakenly thought to be heavy cruisers. Correct identification at this time was vital in order to choose the right type of shells. Prinz Eugen's First Artillery Officer (I.A.O.), Lieutenant-Commander Paulus Jasper, also believed the approaching ships to be cruisers and ordered to load 20.3cm high explosive shells.1 At this point, the British warships (in reality the battlecruiser Hood and the battleship Prince of Wales) were approaching the German battle group on a course of 280º at 28 knots. Vice-Admiral Holland, aboard theHood, familiar with the vulnerability of his battlecruiser in long range combat, was probably trying to get closer quickly before opening fire. Admiral Lütjens did not have any other choice but to accept the combat.Balance of ForcesShip:BismarckPrinz EugenHoodPrince ofWalesDisplacement:51,700 mt19,042 mt49,136 mt44,400 mtArmament: · Main: · Secondary: · Torpedoes:8 x 38 cm12 x 15 cm-8 x 20.3 cm-12 x 53.3 cm8 x 38.1 cm-4 x 53.3 cm10 x 35.6 cm16 x 13.3 cm-Armour: · Main belt: · Turrets: · Upper deck: · Armour deck:320 mm130-360 mm50-80 mm80-120 mm80 mm160 mm25 mm30 mm305 mm127-381 mm38 mm75 mm348-374 mm150-324 mm-124-150 mmSpeed:30 knots32.5 knots29-30 knots28-29 knotsDue to the similar silhouettes of the German ships, at 0549 Holland ordered his ships to both engage the leading German ship (the Prinz Eugen) believing she was the Bismarck. After this, the British ships made a 20º turn to starboard on a new course of 300º. At 0552, just before opening fire, Holland correctly identified the Bismarck at last, and ordered his force to shift target to the right-hand ship, but for some reason Hoodkept tracking the leading ship. Aboard the Prince of Wales, however, they correctly targeted the Bismarckwhich followed in Prinz Eugen's wake a mile or so behind. Suddenly, at 0552½, and from a distance of about 12.5 miles (23,150 meters / 25,330 yards), the Hood opened fire, followed by the Prince of Wales half a minute later at 0553. Both ships opened fire with their forward turrets (4 x 38 cm + 6 x 35.6 cm), since their after turrets could not be brought to bear due to the ships' unfavourable angle of approach. Admiral Lütjens immediately signalled to Group North: "Am in a fight with two heavy units". The first salvo from Prince of Wales landed over and astern of Bismarck. Afterwards, Prince of Wales started suffering the first of many mechanical problems, as "A" turret's no. 1 gun broke down temporarily and could not fire anymore. Her second, third and fourth salvoes fell over Bismarck. Hood's first two salvoes fell short from Prinz Eugenthrowing some splinters and much water on board.Two 38.1 cm shells from the Hood land close to the Prinz Eugen during the initial phase of battle of the Denmark Strait. Photo: PK-Lagemann.Jot Dora! The Bismarck Opens Fire.The British shells were already landing close, but the German guns still remained silent. Aboard theBismarck, the First Artillery Officer (I.A.O.), Lieutenant-Commander Adalbert Schneider, in the foretop command post, requested several times permission to open fire without reply from the bridge. Finally at 0555, while Holland's force was turning 20º to port (a manoeuvre that now permitted Bismarck to identify correctly the Hood and a battleship of the King George V Class), the Bismarck opened fire, followed by thePrinz Eugen immediately afterwards.2 The distance at this time was around 11 miles (20,300 meters / 22,200 yards). Both German ships concentrated their fire on the foremost right opponent, the Hood.Bismarck's first salvo landed short. Aboard the Prinz Eugen, the port 53.3 cm torpedo tubes had already been trained towards the enemy and Captain Brinkmann ordered the Torpedo Officer, Lieutenant Reimann: "permission to fire as soon as in reaching range". At 0556, Prince of Wales' fifth salvo fell over again, but the sixth straddled and likely hit the Bismarck even though aboard the British battleship no hits were observed. The initial fire of the Germans had been excellent, and at 0557, the Prinz Eugen had already obtained a hit on Hood's shelter deck near the mainmast. This caused a big fire which spread as far as the second funnel. The Bismarck had also been hit, and was now leaving a broad track of oil upon the surface of the sea. Therefore, Lütjens ordered the Prinz Eugen (that had already fired six salvoes on Hood) to change target towards the Prince of Wales, together with the secondary battery of the Bismarck which had just entered in action. From the start the Bismarck found herself favoured by the enemy disposition that allowed her to use all eight guns while the Prince of Wales was now firing at Bismarck with only five, and the Hood was erroneously firing at Prinz Eugen. War, Ship, Warship, Water Structure, Ww2, Battleship, World, Boat

Reliable Dropper Elevator Redstone Device Map Most vertical dropper pipes have a comparator only at the bottom dropper, so what happens if no more items enter that dropper, but there are still items in the pipe? - They never reach the top and get stuck within! This design is a little bit bulkier, but it makes up with reliability- it's impossible to jam it, you could throw in 10 000 items and it will shoot out every single one of them, and a modular design so that you can make it as tall as you want.I designed it for a lottery machine where it would have been unacceptable that some of the items could get lost in transfer. SF-8 Daemon Heavy Fighter-Bomber Air Structure Map Aaaand another delay between my uploads! Sorry, since I don't really have an excuse for this one aside from general laziness in making the title picture. No status update on the Dauntless either. Making good progress on the blog/blog ship though, so expect that by May. (I know, long time)Specs!Name: SF-8 DaemonType: Fighter-BomberManufacturer/Designer: Brony AerospaceCrew: 2 (Pilot and Gunner)Length: 28mWingspan: 25mHeight: 7mPowerplant: 1 Mk. Im Cold Fusion Reactor powering 2 Pratt & Whitney VS2000 VASIMR rockets. Brony Aerospace anti-gravity generators.Top Speed: 0.5x lightspeed (sublight), 5x lightspeed (FTL)Range: 50 million miles (sublight), 20 lightyears (FTL)FTL: 1 Mk. X warp reactor powering coils in wing nacelles.Armament:2 Turbolaser Cannons (Output=75 MW)Turbolaser pod interchangeable with 70 MJ railgun.2 Laser Cannons (Same pod as turbolasers, Output=30 MW)2 Missile Hardpoints on wings (Typically antimatter antiship and heavy anti-fighter)3 Bomb baysSpace based Ordnance: Thruster-propelled bombs pushed away from craft, typically nuclear, but antimatter and conventional warheads available.All current Atmospheric Ordnance, nuclear and conventional.1 Auto-guided GAU-8 Avenger Gatling Gun for CIWSShields: Electromagnetic Hull Plating, Carbon Nanotube SkinWeight: 50 tonsManeuvering System: Standard Control Surfaces (atmospheric) and multi-axial RCS system (space)Cost: $50 million Skylord dollars ($100 million USD)Upon the production of the first SF-18s, a need was quickly found for a heavier attack craft to assist with more dangerous missions. Skylord Brony designed the SF-8 as a space-worthy A-10 on steroids, packing as much armament as the average Falcon-class and then some, with maneuverablity and speed nearly matching its little brother. The larger size allowed for a larger and more powerful cold fusion reactor, which in turn powered a warp field generator, making the Daemon the first Skylord military FTL vessel. Unlike the Harpy, it is only capable of landing on airstrips and the Andromeda-class, being too large and heavy for the Dauntless. Also unlike the SF-18, it is currently only available to the Skylord military and civilian market, albiet demilitarized for the latter. Despite these differences, Daemon uses a scaled-up version of the same Pratt & Whitney VASIMR engine in the Harpy, and there are plans to adapt the reactor to fit in its smaller sibling. A spaceworthy and airworthy craft, the Daemon is planned for escort missions, strikes on enemy spacecraft, ground support, and strategic bombing missions before being replaced in that role by a dedicated bomber in development. The Daemon is also the first craft in the Skylord fleet to accomodate railguns, a recent innovation by Skylord weapons contractor Trojan Technologies that made the concept starfighter-sized. The most powerful aircraft in the Skylord Space Fleet so far, it will soon be outgunned by upcoming vessels. Air Structure, Space, Bomber, Fighter, Celestial, Chaos, Starfighter, Daemon

Placeable Chicken Checkpoints (Vanilla) Redstone Device Map I created a little someting in vanilla Minecraft called "Placeable Chicken Checkpoints", what are they?They're checkpoints that the players themselves can place, teleport, destroy, and replace. How many checkpoints & mob is completely optional to anyone who's willing to change it.Let me go into more detail:By using a Blaze egg named "Checkpoint_Creator", I can have a chicken named "Checkpoint_#", teleport the Checkpoint to the Blaze, then kill the Blaze. Now, this checkpoint won't move, take damage, target, or anything. How the player teleports to these multiple checkpoints is completely opitional & changable. For me, it was seletcing the ninth slot on the hotbar and having multiple tellraw commands to do the work.I go into further detail & great explanitions in the video above. I hope you enjoy :) (And if you do: Like & Subscribe!)Examples of what this can be used in:CTMParkourPuzzlesSurvivalPvP Mini-GamesAnd much more!(NOTE: If you do use this mechanic in any sort of way: Please inform me of it! I'd like to know your use of it.) Redstone Device

SpawnCreator for 1.7+ Maps Redstone Device Map Players that joined first-time not spawning at the correct places in your adventure map?SethBling made a contraption long ago, but it was extremely big and might not work as spawning seems buggy ATM.Dragnoz also made a smaller one, but does not seem to work as the scoreboard does not give you 0 by default.Therefore, to ease the burden of building massive contraptions, I have created my own (in a 3x3x3 box!) and decided to share it with the PMC community! Unfortunately, you need to do a bit of tinkering with your world file.How To Use?1. Download the schematic and place it into your world using MCEdit.2. Download a NBT editor. This one by jaquadro works epicly for me.3. Look for your level.dat file using your editor.4. In Data, edit GameType to 0, then save your changes.5. Enter the world and modify the coordinates to the place you want.How It Works:1. Your world is now Survival Mode instead of Creative or Adventure Mode.2. Previously joined players won't be affected; newly-joined players will be survival.3. The device teleports all survival players to the coords you set.4. One tick later it will set all survival players in the radius of the coords to adventure mode.5. It will also set their spawnpoint to that place.Happy building! If you used this contraption, remember to give some credits! Redstone Device

7-Segment Stackable Display Redstone Device Map The map was created in 1.7.2Please report any bugs to me via a comment of the project.Hey guys!I made this 7-segment display that got the number 0-9 encoded in a piston tape. The unique feature is the fact the the display is 4 wide and stackable wich means that there's only one block between each number. To achieve the 4 block wide, I used cauldron, water and comparators instead of the classic solid/non-solid block piston tape. With that trick, I managed to make the piston tape 2x6 (10 columns for the 10 numbers and 2 spaces in 2 opposite corners). I just adapted my redstone to fit the 4 wide and stackable propreties that I wanted for this display.The total dimensions of a single number display is 4x13x10 (Check the MCEdit Schematic).Sadly, the timing for each segment isn't the same but you could adjust that by adding delay with repeaters. Because I wanted the minimum delay, I didn't add them in my displays.This project is only to demonstrate the display and what I can build with it will follow in the next days as separated projects.Shout out to mateuszdrwal for the idea :pPlease refer to this PMC page or my profile if you want to use this redstone device in one of your build, that would be greatly appreciated :)Don't hesitate to leave a comment with your impressions or ideas for other projects! Click to Run Command Block Powered Disease in Minecraft! Redstone Device Map Hello back again with a new redstone device. I was playing around with some grenades and made this curse grenade that if it were to ever be used everything around it would be cursed. Then I got the idea that the curse could spread. It was kinda like a virus. Then i got a great idea. What if there were diseases in Minecraft! So I got right on it and was so exited I accidentally deleted the world with my grenades in it. :( I was thinking of something that could go right with my super mobs "mod". (http://www.planetminecraft.com/project/super-mobs-in-game-mod/)And I needed something to spread the disease so I went with endermites because they dont really do anything. But it didnt really make any sense so I changed it to zombies because in super mobs zombies are pretty weak. So the longer you stay around zombies you are more likely to catch the disease. And this variable that counts how long you are around them doesnt recharge! >:DThere are 3 main spreading phases of the disease:1) This is when you catch the disease. You wont know when you have it until visable signs show up. This is one of the most dangerous phases because say a cow has it and it doesnt show. You go out to milk the cow. Now you have it...Note: The virus spreads faster when you are around something that is infected than when you around zombies.2) This is when it becomes apparent that something has the disease. There will be purple particles floating around them and they for no reason will be hurt sometimes. This is when you know they have it so you either kill them, or stay away. Visable effects for the player is blindness.3) This is the most dangerous phase and is when the infected entity starts losing their skin and alot of health. At some point in this proccess the host will infect everything in a 20 block radius with the virus.Villagers are the most prone to the viruse because they walk everywhere, get up close into eachothers faces, and zombies also come after them. Also iron golems because they go after zombies. I have decided to call this virus cave virus because many times there are clumps of enemies in caves and it would be really easy to pick up the virus in a cave even from a bat. BONUS: When you die from the disease it includes a custom death message! (actually 2 death messages 1 being fell out of the world as the actual /kill death and player rotted away as the command block one)Instructions: Press the button and pull the lever! It might also be desireable to turn of commandBlockOutput. (/gamerule commandBlockOutput false)Please give me suggestions on how to make this disease more morbid because i do have extra redstone space! Redstone Device

Fireball Wands in vannila Minecraft Redstone Device Map Hey guys this is yet again one of my vannila "mods" in minecraft!So this one is one of my favorites so far; It adds fireball summoning wands to your minecraft world.You want to be careful with this one though because if you aim at a downwards angle you will probably blow your face off.This "mod" works like this:When you press the button on the bedrock box it creates a scoreboard. You gain scoreboard "points" whenever you right click when you have a carrot-on-a-stick in your hand. So every few seconds every person with 1 or more scoreboard points summons a fireball above their head. To make it so you are not always summoning fireballs, during the other few seconds your scoreboard points are being set to zero.I do think that this will work for multiplayer but I am not sure.In order for this to work, you must use the latest snapshots of 1.8 (or use the 1.8 version) and have the box be in a loaded chunk.For optimal results please diamond and subscribe :)Oh yeah, sorry about the time limit in the top right corner in the pictures, my computer deleted stuff and got all messed up and I can only play the demo for the time being.Again please diamond and subscribe, you guys are what keep me making stuff. Iron Shields in Vanilla Minecraft [14w28b and up] +World Download Redstone Device Map Hello everyone! Today I have made something I have been wanting for a LONG time: Shields in vanilla minecraft!! At the moment, I have it so the second hotbar slot is the Shield Slot (the only slot you Shield will be effective in), but anyways, the effects of holding the shield are: Resistance 3 (60% damage reduction) and Slowness 3 (its quite heavy, you know). In order to out the shields into your survival world, just copy and paste this redstone device into you world! In order to make your own shield, just craft an iron door, and rename it to Iron Shield (caps are very important). Or, as an alternative, there is a command block that puts a preset shield into your 2nd hotbar slot. If I were to choose, I would go with the preset Shield, because it has special features, such as knockback, AND it makes it more fair for the enemies, as you cannot kill enemies with the preset shield. Also, it would cost no resources, but if you want to make it legit, then by all means, go right ahead. Also, There is now a world download available! If you don't want to go through all of the trouble with the schematic, just download the survival world and you're good to go!Anyways, thanks for looking at this, hope you enjoy! Bye!Schematic Installation Guide - click to showStep One: Download the MCedit SchematicStep Two: Download MCedit (Installation instructions for that will probably be on the MCedit website) (If you already have MCedit, you don't have to download it again.)Step Three: Open up the MCedit Folder, and go into the folder that has "schematics" somewhere in the nameStep Four: Drag and Drop the Schematic into that folder. DO NOT put it in a folder inside of the Schematic Folder (i.e. Mobs In Jars folder)Step Five: Using minecraft, create a new world (OPTIONAL, depends on if you already have a world you'd like to put it into)Step Six: Select "Open World", then go to you .minecraft folder, go into your snapshot folder, go into saves, go to the world you want it in, then select "world.dat" (Sounds complicated, but its easier than it sounds.)Step Seven: Select "Import" on the hotbar, then put the machine where you want. Then, go there in-game, press the button, and there you have it! Minecraft, Redstone, Command, Blocks, Redstone Device, Awesome, Mcedit, Shield

The Wanderer's Cabin [Schematic] [World Download] [Seed] Land Structure Map Hey again! Glad to post another project. This is the first house of a series where the theme is "Simplistic Housing", in which the goal is to not make elaborate modern homes, but simplistic ones that provide only the basic necessities of survival...The Wanderer's Cabin is built and owned by a single man who seeks a self-sustaining lifestyle in the pine forests. He has a kitchen, small living room, and small upper bedroom. He also has two greenhouses, as well as an outhouse. The house is located on a pond with a wide variety of fish, which he sparingly catches so the population doesn't quickly die out.This is all I have to say about the project. The home is equipped with basic tools to begin a survival map. There's also a nice surprise in the grotto across from the house, if you choose to play this way.The resource pack is ModernHD, and the rendering program is Chunky.If you liked this post, please diamond and favorite. Cars in vanilla [23 blocks per second] (1.8) Redstone Device Map There are a few concepts about cars in vanilla Minecraft, but most of them are slow and will only travel 1 block per second or so. In this concept I present a car that can travel 23 blocks per second, which makes it similar to a race car. Did you like this concept? Leave a diamond to show your support!How does it work?It is recommended that you watch the video before reading this. There are several redstone clocks that powers command blocks that will teleport the minecart (car) in a certain direction (X+,X-,Z+,Z-) depending on the selected hotbar slot in your inventory. The rest is basically some mechanics to prevent the minecart from being teleported when the players selected hotbar slot is incorrect.If you have any questions just ask in in the comments!I have added a world download for those who are unable to use MCEdit. Redstone Device Map As part of the Map Maker's Toolbox project I've created a gamerule control panel.It's pretty basic but it comes with a nice visual cue for which gamerules are on or off and it can be placed anywhere in your world without losing its effect.To use it, simply open the schematic in MCEdit and place it anywhere you like.So, if you're creating maps and would like to have a simple but elegantly designed control panel you can download it here.(In the video I refer to my Github repository which is an alternative place to download the schematics from.)A few important things:Does NOT need to be inside the spawn chunks to work.Needs to be the only method of changing gamerules in your world to work as intended.Does NOT need any gamerules to have specific values to work as intended.Feel free to use this anywhere; there is no need to credit me unless you're doing something that focuses around the contraption itself such as creating a video about it. Thors hammer (Mjölnir) in vanilla (1.8) Redstone Device Map You have to love Thor and his awesome hammer, Mjölnir, who has the power to summon lightning! In this concept, I have made Mjölnir in vanilla Minecraft. Did you like this concept? Leave a diamond to show your support!How does it work:It is recommended that you watch the video befor reading this. There is a redstone clock that powers a command block that testfor if a player is holding a golden axe. If the player is holding one, a comparator will power a command block that will summon lightning on any entity except for players, as long as the entity is within a radius of 11 blocks. For those of you who are wondering about how I got the Thunder IV enchantment, I simply added a custom lore to the axe.If you have any questions just ask it in the comments!I have added a world download for those who are unable to use MCEdit! Block disguise in vanilla (1.8) Redstone Device Map There are many concepts and videos about mob disguise in vanilla, but no one has really posted anything about block disguise in vanilla. What you are looking at here is a concept I made for block disguise in vanilla minecraft.Did you like this concept? Leave a diamond to show your support!How does it work?It is recommended that you watch the video before reading this, or else it might be hard to understand. There is a rapid redstone clock giving power to some command blocks. The command blocks are making the player and the zombie pigman invisible, as well as giving slowness to the zombie pigman. The zombie pigman is equipped with a diamond ore as a helmet, and it is named "Dinnerbone" so that it is upside down. This makes it look like there is a diamond ore touching the ground like any other block. The zombie pigman is rapidly getting teleported to the player, making it look like the diamond ore is moving as the player is moving. If the zombie pigman dies, a new one will be spawned. This feature was added because the zombie pigman loses health everytime the player jumps.If you have any questions ask it in the comments! Secure Trading Booth Redstone Device Map This is a Safe, and Effective Trading Booth. Here, players enter on either side, and when both players have enetered the booth to begin trading, you hit the left lever which locks you in. Next, you initiate the trading by hitting the right lever which opens a doorway to the movable chest below between players to allow secure trades. Once both players are done trading they close the door to the trade area, then they BOTH have to agree to leave by both hitting the lever to exit or they cannot exit. Redstone Mechanics #1 Egyptian Redstone Device Map Ever wanted to do an adventure map?Don't gave the redstone skills?No worries! We've got your back!We have created some redstone mechanics that are free to use in your map! All you need to do it download the world, or the MCEdit.Schematic and place it into your adventure map... Couldn't be easier! This is the first installment of the mechanics, however more will be avalible if the idea takes off. The more downloads, the more mechanics we will create.This first mechanic is based around an egyptian theme. The mechanic has two features: a door, or a puzzle. If used as a puzzle, we suggest that the pattern on the roof is removed and clues to the pattern are hidden around the rest of the map. The mechanism is 100% fool-proof. The mechanism works as so:The player entersThe door closes behind the player and their gamemode is set to adventureThe player then enters the code using the pressure plates on the floorIf the code is incorrect, the player is killedIf the code is correct, the door opensThe player then exitsThe door closes behind themThe reason we like this map is because it contains no tripwires, pressure plates (apart from the ones for the code), levers or buttons. The door will not open unless the code has been complete and the player cannot pass through without it. The mechanic can also be turned on and off with the flick of a lever. {Disclaimer} This mechanic was created by MonkeyMaster10 and is free for anyone to use in their map. I ask that if you put it into your map, that you link this thread in the map description... Thanks :) Map, Complex, Adventure, Redstone, Command, Blocks, Redstone Device, Free

Life As A Zombie in Vanilla! [beta 0.2] (release 1.8) Redstone Device Map Hi everybody. Today I made a system that transform you into a Zombie in Minecraft 1.8. This means that:- Zombies, Skeletons, Creepers and Endermen (if you don't attack or look at him) won't attack you- You burn in the Sun- You're always with Hunger IV- You can kill mobs to regenerate your hunger bar- You're always with Slowness III- You're always with Mining Fatigue III- Your head will be, always, a Zombie head, so I recommend you to don't make any helmet!Actually it is in a very beta phase. Any bug tell me, please!How to become a zombie:1. Open MC Edit (you can download it here).2. In your "hotbar" search for "Import".3. Open the folder where The Command Box file is.4. Place it where you want and click the Import button.5. Open your survival world.6. Press the button in the big bedrock box.Note: I'm Brazillian, any English mistake tell me!Olá pessoal. Hoje eu fiz um sistema que o transforma em um Zumbi no Minecraft 1.8. Isso significa que:- Zumbis, Esqueletos, Creepers e Endermen (se você não o atacar/olhar) não te atacarão- Você queima no Sol- Você sempre terá Fome IV- Você pode matar mobs para regenerar sua fome- Você sempre terá Lentidão IV- Você sempre terá Cansaço IV- Sua cabeça será, sempre, uma cabeça de Zumbi, então eu recomendo que você não faça nenhum capacete/elmo!Atualmente o sistema está numa fase beta. Qualquer bug me avise, por favor!Como se transformar em um zumbi:1. Abra o MC Edit (você pode baixá-lo aqui).2. Em sua "hotbar" procure pela opção "Import".3. Abra a pasta onde está o arquivo em Schematic da The Command Box.4. Coloque-a em qualquer lugar e clique no botão onde está escrito "Import" (não o da "hotbar").5. Abra o seu mundo survival.6. Aperte o botão na caixa de bedrock (matriz) gigante. Zombie, Survival, Minecraft, Redstone Device, Vanilla, Schematic, Voc, Sobrevivncia

[MCEdit Schematic] Create Spinning Heads - Map Making Trick 3D Art Map [MCEdit Schematic] Create Spinning Heads - Map Making TrickAs of 1.8, it is possible to create Armor Stands in minecraft. These Armor Stands are quite basic in survival, but in creative, they offer a whole new range of features, including, heads that slowly spin around in a circle. This can be a useful way for displaying characters, or other things as well.Here, i have made an MCEdit Schematic Box, that can generate the spinning head for you. Instructions are on the box for you to follow. After generating it, use /tp @e[type=ArmorStand,c=1] @p and it will teleport it to you (so long as it is the closest armorstand to you.Also this doesn't interfear with other armor stands. It only converts the ones you make in the machine into spinning heads.If you have any Questions/Comments/Suggestions, please leave them in the comments below, or send them in a PM to me.FILE SIZE: 6.49 KB 3d Art, Generator, Creative, Simple, Schematic, Head, Mcedit, Mapmaking

Quad-Directional Counter Redstone Device Map The map was created in 1.7.2 Please report any bugs to me via a comment of the project. This is a 4-directions counter, made of cells that I built to make a cursor on a screen. It is modular but slow, 10x10 cells and you could achieve the same result by making 2 bidirectinal counter and one decoder (it would also be way more faster). You can check the schematic to see what one cell looks like.There are a total of 3 different inpust:First Square (Purple) : If no cell is lit up, you must press the button located at the left of the contraption (You must have no direction selected)Directions (Pink) : If 1 cell is lit up, you must choose ONE lever to make it move (if you choose more than one, the cell will multiply).+1 (Orange) : When you are ready to move the cell and 1 direction is chosen, press the +1 button to move it.Note:If you press the +1 button while no direction are selected, the "board" (I call the board all the cells together) resets. It's a great way to reset if you accidentaly have more than one cell lit up at once.If you try to move the lit up cell outside of the board, it'll just turn off the cell.You must wait 2-3 second after you switch levers to press the +1 button. Else, the cells will just turn off.Please refer to this PMC page or my profile if you want to use this redstone device in one of your build, that would be greatly appreciated :) Don't hesitate to leave a comment with your impressions or ideas for other projects! Redstone Device, Counter, Direction

Time Control! Redstone Device Map This is a very simple project i have made for the latest 1.8 snapshots. It involves freezing everyaspect of development in minecraft with the use of an item. It will prevent the earth from spinning, mobs and other players from moving or attacking, grass and crops and saplings from growing, and can freeze a creeper in the middle of its fuse! This is obviously very overpowered so i created a survival world! This time I ahve used a different faster clock which has invisible redstone blocks. You can tell if it is on or off by the redstone lamp. To turn it on place a redstone block on the redstone block in the center and to turn it off place one on the side block.Fun Facts:If 2 players use the Time Control eggs they will both be free to walk around while everything else is frozen. A frozen player can still use an egg.You CANNOT regenerate health while time is frozen. This means in order to get health back you have to go into real time or use a regenerative item.You cannot pickup items off the ground while time is frozen. Although this is not much of a problem because you can just click the egg twice to quickly pick up items.This doesnt work in creative mode because eggs are a commonly used creative mode item but still can be used to turn off the time freeze in creative mode.You can create lightning or even fat creeper statues by doing this although they will be unfrozen when time is resumed.The thumbnail picture is me have frozen time and for comedy put an armor stand with a pumkpin on its head like the zombies are looking at it.There's not much to this but i found it fun enough to play around with to post. Command Block Gravity+ Redstone Device Map I made this for a server, but I figured I should share it here as well. There's really not a lot to it, it just makes it so that any one block thick dirt or grass falls when you walk on it, like in TerraFirmaCraft for those of you that are familier. It's a tiny 3*3*3 MCEdit schematic that you can just paste into your world. It's fast enough that if your computer is running well, you still take 80% of the damage you would have taken falling the whole way. If the clock isn't working, which you can test by walking over thin dirt, you need to trigger the bottom block, then the top one to restart. Redstone, Redstone Device, Gravity, Terrafirmacraft

Living blocks and ores in vanilla (1.8) Redstone Device Map 1.1.2 UPDATE: Read the update log for more details, or watch this video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JCif_6G8TDgThere are some mods that makes Minecraft blocks come alive, and when you kill a living block it will drop itself. Many people seems to like this idea, so I took this concept and made it in vanilla Minecraft. The living blocks are easy to modify, and they can be any kind of block in minecraft.Did you like this concept? Leave a diamond to show your support!How does it work?It is recommended that you watch the video before reading this. The living blocks are actually zombie pigmen that are invisible with an ore as a helmet. They are named "Dinnerbone" so that the Pigmen are upside down, which makes it look like the ore is walking around. I chose zombie pigmen to be the ores, because they are neutral. They will not attack you if you do not attack them first. There is a rapid redstone clock that powers some command blocks. One of the command blocks will testfor a zombie pigman. Once it can not find a pigman (which means a player has killed it) it will summon a new pigman and also give the player an ore, so if the player killed a diamond ore he/she will receive a diamond. The other command block is just making the pigmen invisible.If you have any questions just ask it in the comments!I have added a world download for those who are unable to use MCEdit. [1.8] 100% Custom Mob Drops Redstone Device Map As part of the Map Maker's Toolbox project I've created a way to have mobs drop 100% custom loot. It supports multiple kinds of the same mob (each one dropping a different kind of loot), chances, different stack sizes of the same item, etc. To use it, simply open the schematic in MCEdit and place it in your spawn chunks. Remove and replace the redstone blocks to start the fill clocks that run this device.So, if you're creating maps and would like to have mobs drop anything you like them to drop, you can download it here. (In the video I refer to my Github repository which is an alternative place to download the schematics from.) A few important things: DOES need to be inside the spawn chunks to work. Can be placed multiple times in one world and work as intended if you set it up to use different CustomNamesGamerules needed to make this work as intended are: doMobLoot, doTileDrops, commandBlockOutput all set to false.Feel free to use this anywhere; there is no need to credit me unless you're doing something that focuses around the contraption itself such as creating a video about it. [1.8] Animated Multiblock Door (Motion tweening in Minecraft) Redstone Device Map As part of the Map Maker's Toolbox project I've found a way to have motion tweening within Minecraft. It supports multiple kinds of the same mob (each one dropping a different kind of loot), chances, different stack sizes of the same item, etc. To use it, open the schematic in MCEdit and place it anywhere in your world. Then use command blocks to create the animation specifically for your door:Spoiler - click to reveal/summon FallingSand X Y Z {Block:BLOCK,Data:DMG,Motion:[0.0,0.0,0.0],Time:0}To animate a block:- set X Y Z to the coordinates of where the block is (Example: 10 64 50)- set BLOCK to what block it is (Example: minecraft:stone)- set DMG to whatever damage value is appropriate. (Example: 1) You may want to change this over the duration of different frames if you're using rotatable blocks (such as stairs)- set the values in Motion for where you want the block to go during the frame. 0.3 is one block, 0.5 is two and 0.7 is three. The second value should stay at 0.0So, if you're creating maps and would like to have smooth animations in them, you can download the basic setup here. (In the video I refer to my Github repository which is an alternative place to download the schematics from.) A few important things: Does NOT need to be inside the spawn chunks to work. Can be placed multiple times in one world and work as intended. Gamerules needed to make this work as intended are: commandBlockOutput set to false.You need to keep at least 1.5 blocks of distance above the animation for it to animate correctly.Feel free to use this anywhere; there is no need to credit me unless you're doing something that focuses around the contraption itself such as creating a video about it. McDonnell Douglas/Boeing F-15E Strike Eagle Air Structure Map After a long while I have finaly gotten motivation to build a new airplane. I present to you my newly crafted F-15E Strike Eagle.Info(Wikipedia)The McDonnell Douglas (now Boeing) F-15E Strike Eagle is an American all-weather multirole fighter, derived from the McDonnell Douglas F-15 Eagle. The F-15E was designed in the 1980s for long-range, high speed interdiction without relying on escort or electronic warfare aircraft. United States Air Force (USAF) F-15E Strike Eagles can be distinguished from other U.S. Eagle variants by darker camouflage and conformal fuel tanks mounted along the engine intakes. The Strike Eagle has been deployed for military operations in Iraq, Afghanistan, and Libya. During these operations the F-15E has carried out deep strikes against high-value targets, combat air patrols, and providing close air support for coalition troops. It has also seen action in later conflicts and has been exported to several countries.Please ask for permission to use in a public project. Thank you. Elemental Snowballs Redstone Device Map Casts 7 elements using snowballs.•Death (Splash Potions of Harming)•Life (Splash Potions of Healing)•Attack (Extra Damage Arrows)•Earth (Dirt)•Explosion (TNT)•Light (Sea Lanterns)•AirTo use, put the schematic into a world and press the green wool button. To change elements, scroll to one on your hotbar and press "1" to go back to the snowball. Ice Maker Redstone Device Map A simple but effective ice maker designed at http://hvplayground.enjin.com/ This build needs to be posted in a biome that has snow. It can be posted on mountain tops to be effective. Be aware that most of this structure is underground. The copy was made from the center of the device, faceing the west direction. This design can be stretched to anysize you need. This was just a small version of the design. Ice is a very useful resource in the game. Just mine with a silktouch pickaxe, then utilize it in your builds rather then bucketing water. After placeing, use a normal pickaxe to break the ice to create water. Use the ice in your farms and spawners for faster travel of items and mobs. Ice may also be used in 2 blocks high x 1 block wide areas for faster character travel. This schematic was built on a version 1.7.9 server. Redstone Device, Ice

Mortissian Heavy Hellflare-Cannon Redstone Device Map Mortissian Defence Inc. presents you the finest cannon you can get your hands on. The Mortissian Heavy Hellflare-Cannon, is a machine-cannon, meaning it fires tnt at very high rates. It's got a 3 turn redstone feed, which allows it to fire at it's such high speeds. The best part is, it's got 4 firemode settings. 0 - Fast, 1 - Medium, 2 - Slow, 3 - Semi-auto Burst. It's all self contained, and works by the magic of command blocks (so make sure commands/cheats are enabled so you can turn the output off [/gamerule commandBlockOutput false]). We at Mortissian Defence Inc. promise that it will greatly help you destroy your enemies with ease. Unfortunately it cannot be aimed (and rotating it via mcedit requires you to change the command to compensate for the changed data values) independently from a ship, but turning the ship itself should solve the aiming problem. We also guarantee that it will fire further than any arrow, so no more trying to hit those tnt catches with flaming arrows. Grumman F7F Air Structure Map A fully functional and realistically scaled replica of the American made Grumman F7F.The Grumman F7F was a carrier-capable twin engined fighter aircraft developed toward the end of World War 2. Medieval Hovel Land Structure Map A small medieval hovel I built on my private server.An introduction to MessyMedieval:Some call it a style, but it's an ideology of having dense meaningful detail.The three aspects of any good build should be 'structural integrity', 'meaningful detail' and 'aesthetical detail'.In each type of build, you'd research what'd be present, what would people use within the confines of the build and you try to represent that, in a visually pleasing manner. It also means the structures themselves need to appear where you'd expect them to be and build to fit the landscape around them, naturally. Symmetry is often left behind for a more natural appearance as it mostly occurs in real life.- Master RobinAre you interested in learning the ways of MessyMedieval?A unique, immersive and detailed way of building in every possible style. Contact (lucas.bleys), Stykernag (Stykernag) or Cirdanoth/Robin (uh-kun) on skype (Skype account names are between brackets). Land Structure

Nomad Survival (The Moving World Border Challenge!) 3D Art Map Welcome to Nomad Survival! A very hard survival gamemode! You have to survive in a 16x16 (Customizable) area, moving in a certain direction!This gamemode can be played alone, or with friends. There's no maximum players, but you should expand the world border size if you play with more than 5 players.Instructions:When downloaded, you will see 3 maps and a .schematic file. To play on one of the pre-installed, just put him in your saves folder and open up minecraft in 1.8 (!). You will spawn in front of (Or underneath when playing on multiplayer, just fly up in that case) a small structure with 2 buttons. Press the Options button first, read the book, configure the options you want, then press the second button like told in the book. Don't stand on the command block structure, let yourself fall down!When you want to put it in a random generated world, just place the .schematic file somewhere high in the sky and do the same as listed above. Tutorial will come out soon!Tips & Tricks:1) Make a shovel! This saves a lot of time when starting a new mine, giving you more time to dig down!2) Don't mine with a wooden pickaxe, this is too slow to stay in the world border!3) Don't mine too low with a stone pickaxe, you will not be able to get distance off the worldborder, so when it comes close you can't escape!4) Always have 2 iron pickaxes with you when mining5) Furnaces are time-consuming, break them on-time before you lose all your stuff!6) Chests are useless.7) In the nether the world border will pause!8) If you have any tips yourself, be sure to post them in the comments!Good luck with this epic survival challenge!~Team Wooloo 3d Art

Put it anywhere in the world, stand on it and put recipe in it to take result.Examples: From: To: To create your recipe you enough to put the Command Block near Redstone Block and enter in it this command:/execute @a[score_CustomCraft_min=1] ~ ~ ~ /testforblock ~ ~-1 ~ minecraft:dispenser -1 {CustomName:CustomCraftingTable,Items:[{Slot:[(slot number),id:minecraft:(name of item)}]}Also you can add more slots to your recipe. Disable Enderman Griefing Minecraft 1.8 Tutorial Other Map Minecraft Tutorial Playlist ► www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLEB388783144C45A8This video will show you how to build a contraption that stops enderman griefing your world by giving them invisible barrier blocks and removing them when placed down by the endermen.Commands/scoreboard objectives add block dummy1: fill ~ ~1 ~ ~3 ~1 ~ stone2: fill ~ ~-1 ~ ~3 ~-1 ~ redstone_block3: scoreboard players set @e[type=Enderman] block 1 {carried:0s}4: execute @e[type=Enderman,score_block_min=1] ~ ~ ~ fill ~-2 ~-2 ~-2 ~2 ~2 ~2 air 0 replace barrier5: entitydata @e[type=Enderman,score_block_min=1] {carried:minecraft:barrier}6: scoreboard players reset @e[type=Enderman,score_block_min=1] blockOptional Commands/scoreboard objectives add timer stat.timeSinceDeath7: execute @a[score_timer_min=100] ~ ~ ~ fill ~-2 ~-2 ~-2 ~2 ~2 ~2 air 0 replace barrier8: scoreboard players set @a[score_timer_min=100] timer 0Gamerule Commands/gamerule commandBlockOutput false/gamerule logAdminCommands false

Recall Eggs! Teleport home with a Scroll of ...er, Egg of Recall! Redstone Device Map All of my favorite Role Playing Games growing up had a system in place for the player to "recall" or teleport home.I have devised a potential method to do something similar with no mods in Vanilla Minecraft.This is a system that could be used in RPG maps or anywhere else.I have posted a picture to give an idea of how small this system can be, I made it more spread out than needed to aid in walking users through how it works. I have placed a sign on or near every command block to educate the user as to what the block does.I will also post the commands that go into the blocks in this post.Commands block commands posted below: you may also just look at them in game!The block on the right gives 10 of the "Recall Eggs" when clicked, this could be hooked up to a dispenser/dropper that does a testfor of a certain level, kills or other stat to "purchase" them, or they could be found in a chest or given to the player.These "Recall Eggs" are empty spawn eggs and you must click on a nearby block with them to use them.I placed an empty enchant effect on the eggs to give them the enchanted item glow, the lore helps instruct the player on their use./give @p minecraft:spawn_egg 10 1 {display:{Name:"Recall Egg",Lore:[Use this item to return home!]},ench:[]}Next is a simple hopper clock, great for running simple machines like this - google or PM me if you do not know how to make one! Looking at the example you will see it is two hoppers built so that they feed into eachother - they pass the item back and forth which can be sensed with a comperator, in this example the comperator activates the next command blocks.These blocks set up a scoreboard and test for a score on that scoreboard.The top block command is:/scoreboard objectives add Recall stat.useItem.minecraft.spawn_eggThis adds an objective to the scoreboard called "Recall" one point is added each time the spawn_egg is used.The bottom block:/testfor @p[score_Recall_min=1]This block tests to see if the player has a Recall score of at least 1 - which they get when the use the Recall Egg.If this score = at least 1 it activates the next comperator which activates the following blocks.The next block is the one that teleports the player back to the town, home, or location you supply.In my example it is: /tp @p 0 69 17You will want to change this to your desired location.The command blocks touching this block are also activated and they are:/playsound mob.endermen.portal @pwhich plays the sound of an enderman teleporting - I thought that this sound suited the teleporting action of the player but one of my play-testers said it scared him every time! - you can use any sound, or no sound at all. Next:/execute @p ~ ~ ~ particle spell ~ ~-1 ~ 1 1 1 1 500This executes the particle command - take a look at the variables of this command to learn more! I used the spell particle effect with 500 particles, this makes a nice little white cloud of poofs at your feet when you land from teleporting. I learned that if you swap this block with the teleport block, making this block activate first then the poof of particles appears at the location you were at BEFORE teleporting which may be appealing in some situations. Use any particles you wish in any amount, or leave this block out for none, it is optional.I next created a tellraw block that informs the player of what happened to them, I have seen similar behavior in RPGs and liked the look of it, it is also optional./tellraw @p {"text":"","extra":[{"text":"You have been teleported home!","color":"green"}]}The player will see "You have been teleported home!" in their chat window, the text will be green. Read more on JSON formatting and the tellraw command, you can do a lot with these functions, this is a very basic example and as I said, optional.After teleporting the player I decided to have the Recall objective removed from the scoreboard which, of course, erases the score as well - the system will not work without this command:/scoreboard objectives remove RecallThe first block I described is constantly being activated by the hopper clock and will re-create the scoreboard objective, with a score of 0 making this system work.The final block in this system removes 1 of the Recall Eggs from the players inventory: /clear @p minecraft:spawn_egg 1 1 {display:{Name:"Recall Egg"}}This is somewhat optional depending on the way you are going to use this system. Without this you can just give the player 1 Recall Egg and it will last forever and have infinite uses. The way I have used it requires the player to somehow get more through gameplay.I welcome any questions or comments - this is one of my first "complicated" (ha) contraptions so I imagine there are better ways some of these things can be done or some mistakes I have made - I love being educated so, feel free!This system will not work in a multiplayer environment, not without modifications, as I make use of the @p command - this would cause all of these things to happen to whichever player happened to be nearest to these command blocks - which could be entertaining but would not achieve the desired effect.I have tested this system from many many thousands of blocks away from the command blocks, however, unless this system is created in the chunk that contains the world spawn it stops working if you walk too far away. Some command block contraptions only need to be active when you are nearby, ones like this should remain constantly running - I do not believe this system causes much if any real lag, let me know if otherwise!You can set your worldspawn to the location of this machine by typing /setworldspawn when standing nearby. This system can easily be hidden under the ground.The lapis blocks seen in the screen shot and included in the schematic have no function, they were placed to keep you from seeing the other things I was working on in the background!As I learn I may update this system - I would like to know how to make eggs unique so that I could use other spawn eggs for different recall locations, or different "spells" as far as I know there is no way to testfor the display name datatag - I wonder if there is another way to make them unique, perhaps placing different non-functional enchantments on the different eggs - if you are educated in this area please spread the love - I would happily credit you in any further work of mine.This system could also be used with other items that share the stat.useItem objective - this includes placing records in a jukebox, potions, eggs, spawn eggs, bows, fishing poles, carrot on a stick, paintings, ender pearls, snowballs an empty map and possibly other items - I have not tested them all!Please enjoy, if you have read all of this please comment and let me know what you think!I welcome it all, good and bad, thank you for your time - also feel free to PM me to teach me or have me teach you! :)Please enjoy this system, you make make any use of it any where in any way without my permission/this is permission.If you WANT to let me know how/where you used it I would love to know!The only thing you NEED/should modify is the block that sets the home teleport point. I placed it on top of the diamond blocks.If you do not change this and go to test the system you may end up somewhere far from this creation depending on how/where you implement this! (I know, I did it to myself and had to find my location in MCEdit!)This system is available as a schematic and as a world upload, you may post those and the screen shot anywhere you want too! Rpg, Command, Blocks, Redstone Device, Magic, Teleport

Earthbending in vanilla Redstone Device Map Are you a fan of Avatar The Last Airbender? In the TLA series some peopel have the ability to bend earth. I have recreated that ability in vanilla Minecraft. Now you can throw earth blocks as well!Do you like this concept? Leave a diamond to show your support!=========================================================================================== It is recommended that you watch the video before reading this. If you are importing the machine to your Minecraft world you may have to give it a "kick start" by replacing the redstone blocks. What the command block does is it is testing for players with the earthbender score set to at least 1. Then they check what direction the player is looking at. Based on the direction a command block will execute an /execute command that summons a FallingSand entity. Another command block will make all FallingSand entities effect all mobs except players inside a radius of 1 block with instant damage. To become an earthbender type this command:/scoreboard players set (player) earthbender 1In order to earthbend (as mentioned in the video) simply select your first slot on your hotbar. To earthbend again simply reselect the first hotbar slot. As an example you can select your second hotbar slot and then select your first hotbar slto again. Ultimate Trade Center Redstone Device Map Ultimate Trade center - IMAGES BELOW OUTDATEDIntroductionThe ultimate trade center is a redstone build that allows players to trade items with each other. It is 100% vanilla compatible and can be used on any type of server, as it is a great substitute for possibly lag causing trading plugins.This project is the outcome of me building a trade center for my SMP server, Jellteks (mc.jellteks.com), and is actively and so far successfully used.FeaturesThis trade center/trade booth is one like no other, as it has multiple features to prevent scam and theft.Theft proofScam proofFastCompactLight indication (Shows if the trading partner is willing to accept the trade)How it worksThis trade center has two sides, one for each player and both with equal controls.Once the players have entered, the doors have to be closed in order for the trade center to work; this is the anti-theft feature and prevents trading with open doors. Note that both players have to activate the door close lever in order for both doors to close, they work together.The players then proceed to throw the items they are willing to trade into the hole containing the water.If both players are happy with the items, they can accept the trade using the other lever. Once someone accepts the trade, the redstone lamp will light up on the other side notifying the trading partner.Once both players accept the trade the iron trap doors will close, preventing the players to retrieve their items before the trade is complete (anti scam feature), and the pistons will retract allowing the water to push the items to the receiver.In the end, both players are encouraged to put all levers back to the default position allowing others to use the tradecenter again.FactsDimensions: 12x13x15Redstone: (Still calculating, coming soon)I hope you guys liked this creation of mine, I will most likely upload a scematic once I get five diamonds on this project :D. If you liked this post, you may like some of my others! Feel free to check them out and subscribe if you like them Redstone, Redstone Device, Multiplayer, Trading, Center, Trade, Essential

Secret Chest Schematic Other Map Secret Chest schematic.To be used on servers, this schematic is a room with a Trapped Chest inside. When a player opens the chest, Tnt falls down from the ceiling and a piston blocks the only exit leaving the player helpless and doomed.Perhaps this is a little sinister, but I found it fun to make.Please leave a comment with your thoughts on this schematic :-)To Load into Server:*YOU NEED TO HAVE THE "WORLD EDIT" PLUGIN INSTALLED ON YOUR SERVER*1. Paste the .schematic file in your /plugins/worldedit/schematics/ folder on your Minecraft server.2. Load it in the game with the command "//schem load mcedit filename", where "filename" is the file name without the .schematic extension.3. Now (In Game) go to where you want to place the schematic and paste it with the command "//paste".4. If the schematic is badly placed, you can cancel it with the command "//undo". :3V1L: Mechanical Motor MK2.1 Redstone Device Map :3V1L:Mr.J0K3R, back again... Today I bring you my slimmed down, & tricked out, :3V1L: Mechanical Motor (or Engine) Mk 2.0! As a request / favor for a friend, I've gone through, & compressed all the features of my first motor into this bite sized Redstone toy. Just like any proper "real world" engine, this bad boy is loud, obnoxious, & actually pumps out a pulsating Redstone signal, otherwise known as "Alternating Current" or AC. All of that noise is generated by the Redstone cycle pumping the 5x vertical "Iron Block Engine Pistons" on the top of the motor, as well as the 4x "Coal Block Engine Valves" in opposing synchronous cycle to the top pistons, allowing the internal combustion gases a free flowing egress or exit from the engine block. It also features "Engine control levers" x 2, for safe ON & OFF operation, with build in indicator light (lamp) behind a protected "White Stained Glass" viewing port. Please pay attention while operating this device, & > < of the posted safety signs. *Updated to Mk2.1 * Had to make a few minor adjustments as the power on / off switches were not working properly. Seems they were causing the motor to lock up with a fully active redstone signal through it. After clearing out the issues, and adding the 6th "iron block engine piston" on top of it, the project is now 100% complete, & working properly. By using the :3V1L: Mechanical Motor Mk 2.0, you hereby agree to the following;As the manufacturer of this Vanilla Minecraft 1.8+ Redstone Mechanical Motor (engine), I can not be held accountable for any "Minecraft work place injuries" that may result from improper use, or failure to abide by the posted safety notices. Any & all "accidental Minecraft workplace character deaths" are not the responsibility of the manufacturer. Be safe, play smart, & do not hold me liable for any character injuries or deaths.You also agree to "try & remember to": 1. Post back any questions, 2. Post back any comments, 3. Post back any concerns, 4. Block Disguise [2.0] in vanilla Redstone Device Map I made a video on how to summon Living Block pets in vanilla Minecraft. This is of course fake, but it shows one of many examples on how you can use this concept. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Lp6tubCAqt8&list=PL0tE7mOFabgJdg5dMZQXb_ILiSO0IncYW On one of my previous concepts I made block disguise possible in vanillia Minecraft. That version of the concept was very buggy, and the blocks did not look realistic. In Block Disguise 2.0 I have made some changes! Now the blocks look like fully sized regular blocks. You are free to break and place blocks, and you can blend in with your surrondings 100%.Do you like this concept? Leave a diamond to show your support!==========================================================================================It is recommended that you watch the video before reading this. By using the 1.8 command /execute I have been able to upgrade my block disguise concept! If you are going to import this machine in to your world using MCEdit you might have to replace the redstone blocks the first time to give it a "kick start".What is going on is that a command block is executing this command: /execute @a[score_disguise_min=1] ~ ~ ~ summon FallingSand ~ ~ ~ {TileID:2,Data:0,Time:0}This command is summoning FallingSand entities looking like grass blocks. By setting Time:0 on the data the falling sand will not become solid and act as a ghost block. The moment a player moves from one place to another the ghost blocks will disappear. At the same time new ghost blocks are spawning on the player, making it look like a block disguise. Since the blocks are ghost blocks this will not be solid, but you still easily hide while disguised. (I did it at the start of my video to show you how well it works). You can configure what kind of block you are disguised as by changing the TitleID. In order to disguise yourself simply type this command: /socreboard players set (player) disguise 1To undisguise yourself type this command: /scoreboard players set (player) disguise 0==========================================================================================Check out the first version of block disguise: http://www.planetminecraft.com/project/block-disguise-in-vanilla-3033811/ Redstone, Block, Redstone Device, Vanilla, Disguise

REWV-26 "Rover" 3D Art Map This the REWV-26 (Reconnaissance & Electronic Warfare Vehicle. aka "Rover"Lightly armored, but fast and low profiled, makes this vehicle excellent for Reconaissance. The REWV-26 is operated by a crew of 2, A driver and A gunner. Both personnel are responsible for EW operations. The vehicle is equipped with EW equipment, an ECM and a hull mounted cannon, next to the driver. Thought it doesn't cover a large area, it certainly gives it offensive capabilties, and in a way, adds another layer of defense.Further adding to its defense, the vehicle uses Stealth (Thermal Camo) alongside its composite, sloped armor and "Grade 1" Metal (I'll talk about Grade 1 metal in a Lore blog)When deployed in recon missions, REWV-26s are always sent in twos. Which means, 4 personnel are sent in. Design note: There is supposed to be a hatch above the gunner, but due to Minecraft restrictions, I couldn't add it inI have 4 aircrafts that are ready to be posted deployed, all I need to do is create a presentation image for each. 3d Art

Some are carpeted and furnished.An elevator shaft that was built for the Lift plugin. Docking bay with 3 submarines for the Ships plugin. There is a short elevator shaft that leads to this docking bay. The life can be replaced with stairs if you don't use the Lift plugin.One large room, which I generally fill with trees and flowers to act more like an arboretum. The ceiling has a layer of sand above the glass. I also place a Stargate in this room for multiverse travel. The red door leads to the docking bay. The teal door leads to the elevator shaft. I think that's all of the highlights. I hope this is useful- thanks! Water Structure, Base, Underwater

Tombstone v1.0 - Vanilla-mod Redstone Device Map » Requirement «This project is made and tested for MC 1.8.1.» Info «Notice that I am unable to do large scale and long running testing of this. Though I have tested it extensively within my means.The contraption is in a bunker deep below ground, right under the world-spawn. A teleporter can bring any player in creative mode there. And any other player to an observation deck.The schematic file is the whole bunker. Ready to be placed in any world with MCEdit.Edit: Notice that when the schematic has been placed, you need to flick four levers off and on to restart the four clocks. They are all marked with a sign.This contraption should always be placed in the spawn area (within 16 chunks from world-spawnpoint).» Design «This is a vanilla-mod. Which I define as a redstone/command-block setup which does the same as an actual mod. It works equally well in single-player and multi-player. It is meant to be a middle-ground between keepInventory, and dropping all ur items on the ground with only 5 mins to pick them back up.When a player dies, it collect the dropped items and put them in a tombstone (one or two minecart-chests). Which the player then have to go back to retrieve.It can not save items which are burned, removed by explosion or otherwise wouldn't survive normally.A Tomb-Finder function is also included. This allow players to search for tombs with a stick in their hand. Which produce a click-sound to follow.Further detailed explanation:When a player die they are placed in a queue. Dead player furthest away is processed first.A "Wait please" text is displayed. And after the tomb (= an invisible marker) is placed they get the text "You may spawn". The wait period normally last a second. If more players die simultaneously the wait time will be longer. If a player spawn with the Wait text up, a tomb will not be created.The tomb will fall until it meats solid ground and is then locked in place.Then the clean-up process start by spawning in the tombstone and a hopper. The hopper might remove a block if the player has suffocated in a block.When there are no items left to clean up within a radius of 16 blocks, the hopper is removed.Clean-up time: ~2:40 mins. for a full inventory (4 armor pieces + 36 stacks of 64xblocks). In most cases it is much faster.Tombstones can not be destroyed. They are removed when the chest is empty or after a set amount of time. The time is default set to 240 mins (can be changed).Hopper can be destroyed (I can find no way of preventing that). In which case the dropped hopper is removed and a new hopper is placed.If there is a power-source next to the position where the hopper is placed, it becomes locked and unable to do it's job. In this case it will end the process when all items on the ground has despawned.If the player die in the void (below or above the world limit) or above a hole to the void, a tomb can not be placed and the player is informed of this.Tomb-FinderThe Tomb-Finder function works within a radius of 1024 blocks from a tombstone.If more than one player uses the Tomb-Finder, they each activate the click-sound from each their tombstone (if they each are near a different tombstone). All clicks are sent to them all at the same time (if they all are within 1024 blocks of these tombstones). It is then undetermined which tombstone's click each user will actually hear. They will only hear clicks from one. But might not be their closest.» Further development «At this time it is not posible to store a players name. If this should change, it might be posible to:Only collect items which the dead player dropped. And not just all items in the vicinity.Lock the tombstone for the owner of the items. So only they can open it.Give feedback message to a player, if any items was collected or not.Only track the one tombstone closest to the player. And not be interfered by other players searching.» Credits «Thanks to all who have taken their time to make tutorials and otherwise document their findings about command-blocks.The texture-pack Paper Cut-Out is used in some screenshots. Redstone Device, Grave, Death, Gravestone

MK's Decimal Adder [16x23x5] Redstone Device Map I came up with this design while working on a binary-to-decimal decoder. In binary-coded decimal (BCD) the numbers 0-9 are represented in binary 0000 through 1001, with 1010-1111 (A-F) being invalid. When an invalid number is encountered, 0110 (6) is added to make it it tick back over to the correct decimal value. For example, 0111 (7) + 0110 (6) = 1101 (D). 1101 + 0110 = 0011 (3). A carry is also produced to the next unit to add 1 to its current value.With my adder as long as the product is less than 20, it can produce meaningful results, which it always will if you're just using the valid numbers. It also tiles for easy carries.Texture pack used in screenshots is OCTABOX by Headaxe. Redstone Device, Binary, Adder, Bcd, Coded, Decimal

Sky Lift Pack Other Map Sky Lift Pack -An aerial work platform (AWP), also known as an aerial device, elevating work platform (EWP), or mobile elevating work platform(MEWP) is a mechanical device used to provide temporary access for people or equipment to inaccessible areas, usually at height. There are distinct types of mechanized access platforms and the individual types may also be known as a "cherry picker" or a "scissor lift".They are generally used for temporary, flexible access purposes such as maintenance and construction work or by firefighters foremergency access, which distinguishes them from permanent access equipment such as elevators. They are designed to lift limited weights — usually less than a ton, although some have a higher safe working load (SWL) distinguishing them from most types ofcranes. They are usually capable of being set up and operated by a single person.Regardless of the task they are used for, aerial work platforms may provide additional features beyond transport and access, including being equipped with electrical outlets or compressed air connectors for power tools. They may also be equipped with specialist equipment, such as carrying frames for window glass. Working Air Warship: Silver Empire Assault Craft With Adjustable Arc Cannons and AA Guns Redstone Device Map A small war airship that can be flown using the Movecraft plugin for Minecraft. All of the weapons work without the plugin, the plugin simply allows the craft to move around.BackgroundIn response to the onslought of new advanced designs facing them on the battlefield, the Silver Empire updated several of their existing warships in a desperate attempt to hold back the tide that was turning against them in the latter parts of the War of Acquisition. One of those was the Assault Craft. Standard Imperial tactics called for formations of Assault Craft in front of the more fragile fighters wings, to screen them from AA fire. However, in the face of improving cannons technology, the original Assault Craft was found to be too fragile to continue to fulfill its role. So it was redesigned with much greater armor than the original, along with improved cannons of their own. Unfortunately, the draw back was that the new Assault Crafts were a full half again the displacement of the originals, increasing the cost and complexity of manufacturing them. Whether or not it would have been better to stick with the cheap and disposable original Assault Craft is a question that is lost to history.Still, the new warships undoubtably represented a huge leap forward for Silver Empire warship technology and would be a mainstay throughout the remainder of the war.Armament2 x static cannon emplacements, one on each wing4 x repeating fire guns around the cockpit Empire, Craft, Redstone Device, Silver, Assault

Level System for Servers and Adventure Maps! Redstone Device Map This system is made for adventure maps or mini-game servers. It allows people to level up when they're playing. Each time they level up, it will get 33.3% harder to level up next time. You can change the odds to any percentage you want. Watch the last two minutes of the video to learn how. For instance, to get level one you need to get 300 XP. If you give players 100 XP every kill, after 3 kills they'll reach level 1. It will then add 33.3% of 300 to 300, so you'll need 400 XP the next time. This process goes on and on. You can change it to add 1/6 instead of 1/3, so you'll end up with 50 being added instead of 400. It works in conjunction with WubbiConcepts XP bar machine. I hope you guys enjoyed the video. If you did, don't forget to leave a diamond. :)Feel free to use this on your server or adventure maps! Have fun! :) Map, Adventure, Minecraft, Server, Redstone, Command, Blocks, Redstone Device
