Orgrim's Hammer Air Structure Map Orgrim's Hammer is the name of the Horde gunship flying over northeastern Icecrown. It replaces the usual ground bases in other zones due to the large Scourge presence on the ground. It sometimes engages in battle against the Skybreaker, the Alliance gunship. So far, neither have been severely damaged, but both are in constant danger of attack from the other and from the Scourge forces on the ground below them. It is named in honor of Orgrim Doomhammer.The latest in goblin zeppelin engineering, the ship is held aloft by two large balloons on either side of it. In order for engineers to service both balloons, and to prevent them from drifting apart, a large metal catwalk links the two together. While the balloons would appear rather delicate and vulnerable at first, a closer look reveals that solid steel hides beneath the crude fabric. Unlike most goblin Zeppelins, which are prone to malfunction, this gunship bolsters a better use of Goblin mechanics, keeping it from malfunctioning, such as the normal Zeppelins are prone to do.Just as the Skybreaker has a large bomb on board, Orgrim's Hammer has an enormous main cannon positioned at the front of the craft, beginning nearly halfway to the back of the ship and ending by poking out of the mouth of a large, stone wolf head, positioned at the ship's front. However, just like the Alliance's bomb, the cannon is not used in-game. There is an interesting glitch involving the cannon o if one flies into the opening and right to the back, they will find themselves stuck, presumably behind a one-way invisible wall o a spot from which the only way to escape is by hearthing.Orgrim's Hammer is also a common sight in the air near the Argent Tournament Grounds in northern Icecrown, as its circuit around the frozen wastes takes it through and around the mountains to the southwest of the Crusaders' Coliseum.Additional NotesThank you WoWWiki.com for the article. Ventilation is allowed by the grooves in its walls. Star Trek Lorimar Class Runabout (Zeppelin mod compatible) Air Structure Map I have created the Lorimar class runabout from a fan fiction community in star trek. This runabout is possibly my best one so far, I have meant to do it for ages, but have never got round to it. This shuttle has so much technology in it that it is practically bursting with it. Full Adder, 4 wide stackable and 2 redstone ticks fast Redstone Device Map After I had seen a couple of calculators/ALU videos, I decided to design a full adder myself but wanted to make it as narrow as possible and stackable (when placed directly next to each other, the carry out of the first has to be connected directly to the carry in of the second one). When I started building, 3 blocks wide seemed to me to be the narrowest way to construct the the entire circuit, adding the one block of air to isolate each full adder, which lead to a total width of 4 blocks.At first I designed one which followed the commonly used circuit of XOR, AND and OR gates which can be seen in the figure down below.However due to the serial XOR gates the "calculation time" was a bit high (6 redstone ticks or more) but when you search on planetminecraft.com for "full adder" one of the first results would be: "Efficient full adder" by CX gamer which is only 2 ticks. There fore I took a look at the truth table:After which I realized how it could be done in minecraft in such way it would only take 2 ticks. Because the Carry out can be done using three parallel AND gates (A&B, A&Cin and B&Cin) where all the outputs are connected together and all the AND gates have two ticks delay. And the Sum can be done using a three input XOR and AND gate, where the XOR gate only will output an high signal when only one input is high (how to do this can be seen in the last image) and the AND gate will only be high when all the three inputs are high.Add some time building, making a lot of use of the properties of glowstone, and it is done. The end result uses the following materials:74 Redstone Wires14 Redstone Torches15 Glowstone3 Repeaters84 Blocks (opaque)Where the repeaters manly are used for increasing the range from where the input can come from, however it does not effect the "calculation time". The red wool blocks represent the inputs and the blue one(s) the output (however the the glowstone block which I labelled "S" could not be replaced with blue wool). Redstone, Redstone Device, Glowstone, Adder

The House Market 3D Art Map If you plan on downloading this, you must read the red paragraph.All economy servers have shops that have a house underneath, or on top. Now, If you are starting a new server, and you are an op, sometimes you don't want to build your own house, or maybe you just want an interesting house in single player. If so, this is the page for you.What I have built is a house that doubles as a shop. It is built to work with plugins allowing players to create shops on half slabs or chests. I believe that Iconomy does this. Anyway, if you don't want to mod your server, there is the classic water exchange system installed. The other side of the exchange area can be accessed from the house. It contains the light-switch, giving the owner the ability to make their store "open" or "closed", the other end of the water exchange system, a dispenser, and a view of the store.Upstairs, in the house, it is mostly unfurnished. I made it like this as so that the user can create a completely customized living space. It is open, so new rooms can be made by dividing the main room up, and also, a large skylight is installed, as well as toggle-able light switch. Outside a balcony looks down on the world below. A great plus to this is that all of the redstone if hidden or on the roof, making it impossible to see without flying.Just a few notes to those who have downloaded:The ground outside the house is interchangeable, meaning you can make it whatever you want to match the environment - sand, water, anything but lava.The shop is in the front, and the house is in the back.The welcome sign and item name signs are just placeholders.Thank you for taking the time to read this article or even download it. It means a lot to me. However, if you download or replicate this project and then make it public in some way (like using it on a server, YouTube video or forum post), please credit me, or link to this page. Even if you change the basic design, plea hint at the fact I made the original version. It took me a long time to make this and I wouldn't like it if someone took it. Again, thank you. Additional NotesAll economy servers have shops that have a house underneath, or on top. Automatic Watermelon Farm Redstone Device Map Hey everyone! This is an automatic watermelon farm that I have been working on. I used a unique design that maximizes melon output while maintaining an easy single input collection system. The bonus to my farm is that it will not cause unnecessary lag like some fully automatic block update farms do. I explain more about this farm in my video.Test Results:After running several tests, I found this farm to be about 97.5% efficient with an average capacity of about 240 melons (about 3.5 - 4 stacks of melons each time).Reversible Signal Generator: http://www.minecraftforum.net/topic/411181-reversible-signal-generator/The reversible signal generator allows the pistons below the watermelons to rise before the water flows when the farm is turned on. At the same time it allows the water to stop flowing completely before the pistons drop down to their original state. This prevents the watermelon stems from breaking when the farm is being used.The original design is a small 3x3 reversible signal generator. To change the duration between each input change I simply added many repeaters until I found a timing that worked with this farm. Special thanks to Hans Lemurson for this device!Enjoy! Redstone Device, Farm

Simple Chest Trap (GREAT FOR ADVENTURE MAPS!) Other Map Okay, its pretty simple, but just in case anybody needs to know how it works, you just put a row of signs attached to each other behind a block that's on top of a chest. Now if this is on an adventure map, and the player sees the sign FOOD written across the chest, they will open it for sure. This breaks the signs all the way down the row, dropping them on wooden pressure plates, that then power the TNT. It does not NEED to blow up, it could for example trigger a dispenser to shoot a whole bunch of creepers out or something, but that's up to you. NOTE This must be backed up to a wall as shown, or the player will easily disarm it. Adventure, Chest, Bomb, Trap

Proximity Based Land-Mine (GREAT FOR ADVENTURE MAPS! Redstone Device Map Bury one of these puppies and watch the dead bodies fly, This bomb is 100% stable, and it WILL NOT BLOW UP until a player is within it's range, guaranteed. When you get close to it, cave spiders spawn inside, and jostle each other around until one bumps the side of the fence, and then lights the TNT. Other, more simple proximity detectors have a good chance of blowing up before a player reaches it due to natural spawning of monsters, but this works off the fact that spawners can create spiders mid-air, while the half-slab and fence flooring creates a spawn free zone. This means that there is a 0% chance of it blowing up unless you modify it in such a way that it leaves it open for monsters to climb in. Placing a redston lamp above the spawner can allow you to turn the bomb on and off, Given that it is a player detector, it does not HAVE to blow up, you could wrap redstone wiring around the fences for another use (or you could always add a bigger payload of TNT :3.) Land, Redstone Device, Tnt, Player, Bomb, Detector, Proximity

Big code door, with built in security. Redstone Device Map Have you ever gotten tired of people flipping your switches over and over again, with seemingly no end in the near future? Your solution has arrived! Simply place this at the bottom of a stairwell, input the proper code, pull the lever labeled ENTER, and the doors open. If the code is wrong and the lever is pulled, the floor will disappear and drop the victim into a pool of lava, where he/she will be crushed and burned to death. Its complex enough that XRAYers without extreme experience with redstone wont find out the code, but also easy enough to build in a matter of minutes. Its at the bottom of stairs so that the victim has a greater chance of standing on the trap. This can be altered, but wont be NEARLY as effective (I've done experiments on my server with it. ;3) Changing the code is as simple as placing a redstone torch on top of the levers to be flipped downwards, and redstone on top of all others. (NOTE: The trap will only be activate if the ENTER lever is pulled with a WRONG code, as to not scare away the victim.) Redstone Device, Door, Code, Hidden, Trap

One-Way Non-Recessed Piston Door (can be reset) Redstone Device Map This is a one-way door that requires resetting after each use.- Can be put to good use on a server if you require a quick and sealed way out of a house.After running on the pressure plates, the pistons will close behind you and cover up the entrance you just passed through. Lighting will come on within 2 seconds of the passage closing. The end of the passage has been left open for you to build whatever you please.To reset the lighting, there is a button in the room outside. If you want to move the reset button to a more appropriate place, just rewire the single wire behind the original button to where you want it.To reset the door, break the pistons that closed behind you and replace the (wool) blocks that make up the wall. Then replace the two pistons either side and the two (wool) blocks either side as well.This isn't a brilliant contraption, but it works, and feel free to alter it and/or send me some suggestions! Redstone Device, Piston, Door, Way, Non, Saferoom, Sealed, Recessed

MCS (ModularCartStorage) Minecart Map This not only looks cool but is highly functional. Each collum contains 9 storage carts. at the press of a button. the bottom cart is sent to the top, giving your base a SUPER compact storage room (wall :D). This is perfectly suited for underground bases. My current setup has a organization system; the block the button is attached to represents what types of items should be placed in the carts in that row. You can change this if needed. To have more carts in a collum, just remove the rails, and build the part touching the pistons a little bit longer, to heighten it. These can be placed next to each other indefinitely. Due to a North-South quirk, the rails might glitch up if the schematic is rotated. Have fun with it, improve it, whatever. No credit is needed, I want to see what the community can use this for. Minecart, Storage, Dispenser, Pez

Extremely Compact Secure Combo Lock. (Best Combo Lock) Redstone Device Map This is my extremely compact and secure combination lock. It only has one single lever but allows for 33 million different combinations.A more in dept video of this build will follow soon: In the video I will also show you how simple it is to set your own combination. How to set your own combination: www.youtube.com/watch?v=qE6x5A4bmbo ------------------------------------------------ Dimensions:Input Circuit Dimension: 5x8x12Main Body Dimension 10x12x12------------------------------------------------Color Description for all the circuits.Limegreen Circuit : Controls the direction of the feed-tape and stops the feed-tape for a short while before restarting it again.Lapis Lazuli Circuit : Sends a short pulse just before the feed-tape is about to start to start up again. The pulse is used to read the binary feed-tape. After it has read the tape it sends a binary output towards the decoder.Light Grey Circuit : Whenever you stand on the pressure plate this line sends powers to activate the clock that the feedtape runs on. The power can be cut by the lime wire.Purple Line Circuit : This is the toggle-able clock that gets activated by the grey line. It is a 4 tick clock with a 1 tick pulse. The signal gets split and send to directional input that can get toggled by the lime line.Orange Line Circuit: Powers the counterclockwise movement of the feed-tape.Yellow Line Circuit : Powers the clockwise movement of the feed-tape.White Line Circuit : Distributes the output from the feed-tape towards all the different decoders.Dark Green Line Circuit: Reads the direction feed-tape is spinning and turns it into a 1 or 0 state that also gets send into the decoders.Black Circuit: These are the 5 decoders for the 5 hardwired ordersensitive input colors and directions. Each can read a 5 bit numbers giving 32 possibilities per input. So 5 inputs means 32x32x32x32x32 different combinations which is good for a total of 33.554.432 different combinations.Magenta Circuit: This is the RS nor latch array. It is sequential meaning one latch can only be activated if the latch before it is activated. The Latch is setup in a way that it does not allow the same signal to be send into the same latch twice.Pink Line: This is the reset circuit which resets all the latches is the magenta circuit.Red Line: This is the override circuit. Whenever a latch isn't suppose to be activated but a decoder does have the right combination the red line overrides the decoder causing a reset signal to be send to all the latches.Brown Line: This circuit is needed to allow for 00000 to be a possible number for the decoder. This line toggle the red circuit overriding any input in the decoder. Whenever the feed-tape gets read it unpowers briefly removing the override signal that the red circuit is sending into the decoder.Light Blue Circuit: This is the last latch but is actually a t-flipflop. There wasn't any space for a latch. This flipflop when triggered overrides any signals coming from last decoder. So whatever color you enter at this point will reset the whole thing.Cyan Circuit: This is the output of machine. Redstone Device

Compact 3D Printer Redstone Device Map ---------------------------------------------------------------------------THIS IS A CONCEPT, NOT A FLAWLESS DEVICE---------------------------------------------------------------------------This is a basic concept, where I used the idea of the self-repairing floor/bridge to create basic 3D models. Then, post it on PMC or YouTube, giving me appropriate credits for the concept.I'm sure that some genius out there will make this into something far larger. (Please do not replace the stone generator, speed up clock, etc. just to take out my credit)Additional NotesIn the title, it notes that it is compact. compared to the character, it is not very compact. However, comparing it to other printers/ devices of similar concept, it is VERY compact.------------------------------------------------------------IMPORTANT---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------THE CONTROL PAD IS ACTUALLY INVERTED IN THE NE/SW DIRECTION! piston elevator Piston Map An elevator powered by pistons! basically the same thing as my piston monorail, only vertical. Never will you have to walk up boring staircases again in minecraft!Please read these notes before downloading this!*BUILDING ITThe schematic contains 3 parts, the departure section, a mid section and an arrival section, divided by layers of clay. Remove the clay first, then put the departure section somewhere, copy the mid section on top of that as much as you like, then put the top on.it might also be usefull to put some water at the bottom, so you can jump down again.*USING ITthere are signs in the bottom section showing where you have to place the pistons, place any movable block on top of those and press the button. Make sure your remove them again when you reach the top, or the next player might be crushed! If it's a long way up two players can use the lift at the same time. Redstone, Piston, Elevator, Transportation, Lift, Vertical, Method

Piston monorail 2.0 Piston Map I had a lot of positive reactions on the piston monorail, so i decided to make an improvement! This one goes in two directions, just build one train and go back and forth as much as you like!This one is a bit more trikcy to build, so definitely read these notes first!*BUILDING ITThis schematic contains 4 parts, 2 edge sections, and 2 mid sections that you can copy as much as you like. Make sure all the parts are leveled and flush! you should get one straight block in the end.*USING ITPlace a normal and sticky piston facing toward eachother, put about five blocks behind those, and then put another sticky and normal piston, vica versa. The train can in theory take off anywhere on the track, but this may not always work properly. Also do not build much more or less than 5 blocks in between the pistons.Additional NotesAlso check out the 3.0 version: http://www.planetminecraft.com/project/piston-monorail-30/ Redstone, Transport, Train, Piston, New, Ways, Tram, Monorail

7408 quad 2-input AND IC Redstone Device Map This is a common 74 series Logic IC. I have created it for future purposes and all those who are creating minecraft computers, CPU-s, ALU-s or anything related to digital technology. This is my first project and I will continue to contribute.Now on the important part, the 7408 IC has four (thus called quad) AND gates with two inputs (Input A and Input B) and one output. For those who don't understand what it works, it is basically doing AND logic function, meaning only when both input A and input B are in 1 (in our case when the power is on in the specific input) output will also become 1. It was supposed to have 14 input/output gates but we don't really use ground (mass) inhere so you won't be needing it. However 3x4=12 but you can notice that is actually has 13 gates. The 13th gate is actually the power input. You can switch IC on/off with that gate as sometimes you won't be needing it. If you don't need it just replace the switch with redstone torch and close the wall. Redstone, Redstone Device, Gate, Digital, Logic, Computer, Electronic, Cpu

[DISCONTINUED] The Outbreak - Zombie Apocalypse Adventure Map Challenge / Adventure Map [DISCONTINUED]Unfortunately my .minecraft was abruptly deleted due to corruption of multiple files, and I have lost all of my work, including the saves of this and other worlds. I am very angry and sad about this, and I'm sure you are too.Welcome SurvivorsThe Outbreak is a Zombie Apocalypse Adventure/Survival Map, based in Randell City, a once sprawling metropolis booming with towering skyscrapers and happy residents, but now a zombie infested wasteland. Battle hordes of the undead with scavenged supplies, or explore the vast city looking for anything of interest. If you look forward to this map, remember to Comment and/or Diamond and/or Favorite if you want.It keeps me encouraged to do more!For a little bit of an Outbreak preview, feel free to download the Greenhouse Schematic for MC Edit, which I will hopefully incorporate into the map!Remember Survivors... Kill or be Killed!Click to revealOn 23rd August 2003, the zombie virus known as The Outbreak hit Randell City and all it's inhabitants. This infection spreads like wild fire, converting anyone it comes into contact with and has claimed the lives of over 1,000,000 people. Few people remain, like myself, fighting for their lives in the abandoned city. Random supplies have been dropped around the city, and many of us don't dare to go and scavenge them. All of us that are left are either holed up in shelters or out fighting, trying to find anything valuable, like water and food. I've only got a few magazines worth of ammo left, a couple bottles of clean water and some essentials. Soon, I'll have to go outside, risk myself for what's left out there.If anyone receives this, tell the world about this tragedy, put the city in lock-down so it doesn't spread, and most importantly, if it does get out; stay together, fight strong and don't give up.My name is Andy Gilbert, and this is my message.Additional NotesAmazing Texture Pack by Santiago3: www.minecraftforum.net/topic/787848-32x32-14x-the-end-is-extremely-nighvanilla-over-200000-downloadsfeatured-on-curse/ City, Zombie, Survival, Adventure, Challenge, Outbreak, Virus, Apocalypse

8 Minute Infinite Day Clock Redstone Device Map While messing around in my redstone world. I got tired of always resetting the world time and at the time I couldnt get any mods since I was experimenting with snap shots.So to combat this problem I made us of an older technology and combined it with the new command blocks to make an infinite day clock.It is made up of 3 different sectionsA 6 Second Repeater ClockA 10 Block Piston Tape (Which pulses with each rotation of the Repeater)A 8 Block Piston Tap (Which pulses with each rotation of the 10 Block Piston Tape)The result of the above is a compact 8 minute clock that every 8 minutes sends a pulse to a command block in which will set the day time to 1000ticks, ensuring it never goes night.Download and use if you want anywhere. I find it extremely useful in keeping it day as well as allowing me to keep easy-hard difficulty on without fear of them spawning in the night.Diamond/Download/Comment - If you make a better design, please share it with me! :D- Samongi Redstone Device

Analog Clock Redstone Device Map This is an analog clock that will fit in a building and can run in real time or at a minecraft time rate. It has an hour chime.The schematic has an 13x11 block footprint and is 20 blocks tall. There are buttons on front to adjust the hour or minute hand position. The back has a lever to choose realtime or minecraft time, and a button to start the minecraft time timer if it gets stuck.This will easily fit in a building or tower and is readable as a normal clock. The diamond block represents the end of the hour hand with the gold block represents the end of the minute hand. The minute hand moves every 5 minutes in real time mode, and in minecraft time mode it moves every 4.166 seconds. There is a clever circuit to get this fractional tick rate accuracy over a period of time by using a 4.1 second delay followed by two sets of 4.2 second delays.Additional NotesYoutube video showing how the clock works: Redstone Device

[Tutorial] Chair You Can Sit In! Redstone Device Map Hullo there. For all of you out there who value both function AND aesthetics (like me), here's a chair. That Minecraft characters (and mobs too, I suppose) can actually sit in. There's also a schematic in case you want to copy and paste them into your map.Here's a tutorial, if for some reason you can't figure it out. Scroll down to see it.........TUTORIAL1. Dig two holes and place down two slabs of DOUBLE half-stone slabs.2. Place another double half-stone slab on top of one of those.3. Place a single (NOT DOUBLE) half-stone slab on top of the double slab you just placed.4. Place a rail on the unused double slab.5. Place a Minecart atop that rail (it will either not be visible, or very difficult to see.)6. In order to stop the minecart from sliding off the rail, there needs to be a block in front. Most likely some sort of table or desk of some sort. I'll leave the design up to you, but personally I love using piston tables.Alternatively, you can use my schematic to copy and paste them en masse. Enjoy plotting world domination. Tileable Compact Score Display Redstone Device Map An easily built redstone display, completely tileable.When active the wool block is pushed out and the redstone lamps are turned on, when off the redstone lamps go out and the wool block is retracted. Can be used for scores, progress bars, status on BUD devices etc.Size: 5 x 1 x 6Resources needed :2 x Redstone Lamps1 x Sticky piston3 x Redstone Repeaters5 x Redstone Lamps10 x blocks for circuits and outside1 x coloured block as part of the output.Some examples of how to integrate into your designs from what I've used it for:Keep it small and sit it next to other teams for easily read score displays...Or you can integrate it into a team building display....Video soon to showcase it actually moving... Cardinal Light Assault Ship with working machine gun Air Structure Map The Cardinal Light Assault Ship is the main, well, light assault ship in the United Nations Space Navy and the Grand Fleet of Justice in the year 2369. It is armed with one fully automatic dispenser cannon, which fires a maelstrom of arrows and fire charges. The machine gun fires up to 1500 rounds per minute (900 arrows, 600 fire charges).The ship is 24 meters (blocks) long, 15 meters (blocks) wide, and 10 meters (blocks) tall. The ship is controlled by one crew member, who pilots the craft and operates the machine gun. To switch the machine gun on and off, pull the lever to the side of the pilot's seat. Enter the Cardinal through the piston hatch on its top (pull the lever on top of the craft to open this).Additional NotesHow to Use This Ship: Unzip the .zip file or open it with a program such as WinRAR or 7 Zip. Place the Cardinal Light Assault Ship.schematic file in your MCEdit -schematics folder. You may use this ship on a single player world. Compact Tekkit Extractor Land Structure Map Heyo. I got into tekkit.....and I've decided to start a series of machine and machine groups.Here we have a compact Extractor that is completely overflow safe. It is very fast and runs off high voltage solar power. Feel free to tear it apart if you want to know how it works more. This is the first in a series! I will TRY to put out at LEAST one every day. Let me know which ones you want me to do, I will run out! I learned a ton about Tekkit and machines over the last month, so I can pull it off if you want me to.Now for the part you need to read,READ THIS THING. IT IS IMPORTANT.I understand you are going to be confused about installing this.It is compatible with tekkit 1.0.1.3 AND ABOVE. (minecraft 1.2.5 in tekkit). If anything in later version is removed, I will put an update here saying that it wont work because something was removed.To install......You need to make sure you can locate your saves folder on your computer. It is found in %appdata%You then go to roaming, then to .techniclauncher, then to tekkit, then to saves. Plop the zip file in, or extract it to there. Make sure the folder is in there, not just the zip.For the schematic, you are going to have to find your tekkit world in the same location but within MCEdit. Then just open it, plop it in, and ta da. You save it and you have it.IF YOU COMPLAIN, PM ME OR COMMENT ABOUT IT.And as always,Stay SaxxyAdditional NotesTo work the machine, just plop your goods in the goods chest and they pop out as rubber.(check the wiki to find what can be put into the chest). Portcullis by DarthRiddle Redstone Device Map This is a large 12 block opening gate that can be opened from both sides of your castle, city or other creation. The portcullis is bare so you can build around it as you see fit. Take a look at teh video and pictures.For new crafters who want to read some background as to why we like these devices fpr castle raids and PVP maps they can be highly customized to kill your opponents. I pulled this text from wiki:A portcullis (from the French porte coulissante or gliding door) is a latticed grille made of wood, metal or a combination of the two. Portcullises fortified the entrances to many medieval castles, securely closing off the castle during time of attack or siege. Each portcullis was mounted in vertical grooves in castle walls and could be raised or lowered quickly by means of chains or ropes attached to an internal winch.There would often be two portcullises to the main entrance. The one closer to the inside would be closed first and then the one farther away. This was used to trap the enemy and often, burning wood or fire-heated sand would be dropped onto them from the roof or murder-holes. here were often arrowslits in the sides of the walls, enabling archers and crossbowmen to eliminate the trapped group of attackers.As always if you have a request from me, comment below and leave me a diamond if you like the build.Cheers, Redstone Device

Spider Jockey Spawner v.1 by Zertolurian Complex Map Since I haven't seen anyone make a "spawner" for spider jockeys, I decided to make one myself.Its design is based on the facts Spider Jockeys only spawn from **unedited** spawners (i.e. making one spawner spawn spiders every tick won't spawn jockeys), the only difference between jockeys and spiders are their hitboxes (all motion/block collision is based on the spider), and "naturally spawned" spiders have a 1% chance to spawn with a skeleton. So here's how it works: Spiders spawn then fall onto the water, then gets brought to a corner where there is a 1x2 passageway with tripwire above and lava at the end. When a regular spider goes in, it gets incinerated by lava but if a jockey comes along, the tripwire would retract the floor using sticky pistons for a split second, long enough for the jockey alone to fall down alone without extra spiders (most of the time). The jockey then swims in another stream to a single collection point for all the corners. I think it spawns 2 jockeys per minute, if I did the math correct.To make them spawn faster, just duplicate one and link the collection streams(but it might be laggy).To make the spider jockey stronger/become some sort of a boss, you can just use infinite duration potion spawners and equipment spawners (I also found out that **all** undead mobs would equip armor and weapons if the item came out of thin air (i.e. spawners/commands, there is only a slight chance for an undead to be able to pick up dropped items))Feel free to give some points of improvement or use it for your map or whatever, just give credit. :PJust wanted to post this thing here because, what's the use of a creation if you don't share it? :D Complex

Compact 3x3 BUD Switch - Redstone Redstone Device Map My good friend Xenocides and I were messing around with some redstone the other day when I came up with this neat little 3x3 Wall BUD (Block Update Detector) switch. It works on the basis of diagonal redstone powering the piston.This could be used as an indication that your furnaces have finished cooking, redstone ores have been activated, etc...I used the principle of diagonal redstone. This is when you power a piston diagonally from a block and trick it into thinking that its being properly powered, when it in fact isn't.The reset mechanism then kicks in after we update the piston where the block that is diagonally placed to the piston is then unpowered, resetting the piston.A simple compact and neat 3x3 design using minimal materials that can easily fit into your base. I believe Avalanche_Ali has made an even more compact design. Redstone, Block, Redstone Device, Small, Noteblock, Youtube, Diamonds, Compact

23k Fireworks Display Redstone Device Map this device shoots off 23040 fireworks in 7 minutes 46 seconds and is preloadeddesigned this myself took about 2 hours on figuring out how to lay the redstone so the clock was timed right.you may adjust the repeaters to change the timings but it isn't recommended because doing so may lag your server (the combination of the redstone clock and the fireworks can put stress on your server).ROTATING THE SCHEMATIC NOT RECOMMENDED DUE TO DISPENSERS NOT ALIGNING RIGHT WHEN DOING SOi recommend using a plugin called RandomFireworks to fill the dispensers.bug in worldedit causes dispensers to come out of alignment you may need to realign the dispensers to dispense into the lava.make sure dispensers are facing right direction or you will not get proper results. worldedit has issues placing any redstone device in proper position. the dispenser must face the lavaAdditional Notesyou need at least 1gb ram on your server to run this device.also because this uses a redstone clock make sure you power it down when finished or you could cause other players to lag in the area if left running for long periods of time. Automatic Self-Populating Chicken Farm Redstone Device Map I developed this chicken farm based on the new hopper from snapshot 13w01a. The farm consists of two parts, an egg farm on top and an incinerator on bottom.The egg farm works by placing chickens in a pen with a floor made of hoppers connected to a dispenser, which then shoots eggs into a lower pen. The chickens that hatch will gather around the back wall of the pen underneath their parents. Opening a gate to one side of the back wall allows the baby chickens to climb a staircase and rejoin their parents in the upper pen, thus increasing the population of the farm.Eggs have a 1/8 chance of spawning a chicken, so it takes a while to build up the farm's population if there are only a few chickens to start. Over time, the population will grow exponentially.The second part of the farm is an incinerator built into the bottom of the lower pen, which uses four dispensers to flood the pen and push chickens into the incinerator in the center. A piston in the incinerator pushes the chickens out of the fire to die on top of a hopper, which dispenses the feathers and cooked chickens into a chest below.This build is 17 blocks wide, 25 blocks long, and 20 blocks high, and uses over 700 blocks. I'm currently working on a more compact version that uses fewer resources.NOTE: The world save was made using snapshot 13w02b, since the hopper is not yet available in the stable release channel. Download the snapshot here (if you don't know what to do with it, read the Minecraft Wiki).Watch the tutorialFollow me on Twitter!Like me on Facebook!Like my work? Show your support with a diamond and/or by favoriting it! Redstone, Redstone Device, Chicken, Farm, Automatic, Selfpopulating

The Biomesphere Air Structure Map This is a structure that was used as my server's spawn, for the first five months of it's existence - It's a great design if you have multiple worlds, or portals to multiple biomes, like we do. It looks best over water, but as you can see, looks fine over land, as well. Be sure to stand about 15 blocks under where you want the bottom of this to be, in the exact middle. It's rather big, and will crash smaller servers, when pasted and undone.This was made almost entirely by hand - the only world edit used was to make the original spheres of stone, and fill the bottom of the main sphere with grass.The "DNA strand," in the middle was done by one of my Super Admins, Carcanis. If you play LoL, and his name looks familiar - Yes, it is the same person. He's a LoL junkie, as well.This is 90% my design, licensed under a Creative Commons, and anyone who uses it without permission and proper credit, will be reported. (The other 10% goes to Carcanis, one of my best SuperAdmins, for the beautiful double helix, in the center, along with his help decorating the biomes.)Texture used is my own edited version of Dokucraft - I can't provide a download, but I will send the file to you, over Skype, if you want yours to look the same. [1.4.7] Working Redstone Lighthouse Redstone Device Map This was a lighthouse design I cooked up for the survival server I was playing on. It's designed to fit in a Towny plot which on this server was 16x16. The light actually rotates around the 4 sides. I came up with this lighthouse when a friend on the server show me a piston clock he had made. Since my base was built on a dock, I decided to see if I could use the clock in some sort of lighthouse design and this was the result.On the server, the lighthouse would break when I left the island I built it on, I think this is do to the way redstone will stop functioning when a chunk unloads to increase performance. That is just my guess though. So, some of the redstone will stay powered and freeze because of this.To combat this problem, I wired up a switch mechanism to it so you can de power it and then re power it to get it functioning again without having to get up to the top and break in to replace parts. I tried to make it all as compact as I possibly could with my knowledge of redstone. I would have used a slab ladder instead of a torch one to remove the delay but I wasn't sure how to power all 4 torches at the top with a single slab ladder and their wasn't enough room for 4 of them without redesigning the tower itself.Additional NotesTexture pack: Faithful 32x32www.minecraftforum.net/topic/72747-faithful-32x32-pack-updateredpower2-147/ Light, Redstone, Tower, Ship, Redstone Device, Lighthouse, Piston, Dock

BUD Activated Obsidian Generator Redstone Device Map This was an accidental discovery I made tonight. I am not even entirely sure how it works but I am assuming it works like BUD switch.When you place redstone down, the dispensers activate automatically twice. Once to dispense the lava and another time to suck it back up. This is actually quite cool cause it allows you to make obsidian faster since there's no need for you to hit the input after placing the redstone.Now, I wouldn't be surprised if this has already been documented by someone else, I haven't seen it and I literately just accidentally made it on a SP world while trying to make one that worked with an input and a basic circuit.Either way, for those of you that didn't know about it, enjoy a schematic to play it, for those that did, sorry to waste your time.Additional NotesIf anyone could enlighten me on how this works or knows of any designs that are smaller/more compact, please let me know!Also, I'm not sure if it will work if you download it, it might just be a fluke. I've since tried to replicate it and can't seem to get it to work on any new builds. So, let me know what your experience was. Generator, Redstone, Redstone Device, Water, Lava, Dust, Switch, Bud

Piston Ground Door Redstone Device Map A piston door design I plan on using in survival on a large underground complex.Has 1 locking mechanism so you can lock the door in place (either open or closed) from the inside. This prevents mobs or players from using the switches on the outside while you're in your base. It only cuts power from the outside switch so even with the lock on, you can open and close the door from the inside. I added a redstone lamp to act as a locking signal. When on the lock is on, when off, the lock is off.I color coded the wiring and lined the stairs with glass so you could see inside.Blue: T Flip-FlopRed: Door PowerGray: Outside switchOrange: Inside SwitchGreen: Locking Mechanism Ideally, the top glass above the door would be grass or some other natural block to kind of conceal the entrance and the gold door replaced with something less noticeable.I tried to make it as compact as I could. I believe this is small as it can get in it's current form. Maybe you guys can download it and prove me wrong! I'd love to see more compact versions or anything you make from this.Additional NotesPreview image renders created with Chunky:Chunky on MinecraftForumChunky Wiki Survival, Redstone, Redstone Device, Piston, Door, Chunky, Dust, Circuit

Victorian House + Download Land Structure Map Hey there, this is a Victorian style house that I built today and thought I might share it. I usually do medieval builds, which you can find on my youtube channel, but I wanted to branch out a little.I used only birch wood for the exterior and interior walls (mainly because I needed a wood that had some form of paneling on it.) The problem I ran in to though was that the wall was very monotonous and so to break it up I threw in some turns, as you can see from the pics. I used spruce wood for the roofing. For the pics I used Ovo's Rustic texture pack and I highly recommend it when viewing the house. Automatic Potion Brewing Machine Redstone Device Map Using the new Hoppers in Update 1.5, I made this Automatic Potion Brewing Machine.You just have to load hoppers on top full of the ingredients (signs tell you which ones go where), and fill another hopper with water bottles, and it is ready to go. You can just go down in front, pick the type of potion you want, choose if you want it stronger, increased duration or if you want it to be a splash potion, and press the start button. About 2 minutes later, three of that potion will be in the chest at the front of the machine for you!This works but using hoppers the fill a brewing stand with water bottles and the ingredients to brew the potion you want. A piston loop timer (using the new redstone block) is used to drop out the right ingredients in the correct order to make the potion. There is a large glass wall that allows you to view the items being dropped into a stream of water that takes them to the brewing stand. A tone plays when the machine starts, and when it is done, as well as a light indicating that the machine is running. There are additional warning lights that how if you are out of water bottles or other ingredients, with lights at the filling area showing exactly which ingredients are missing.Additional NotesThe schematic is just the basic machine itself. It can be made into a building, and already has internal stairs to get to the filling platform (and a door can be put before these stairs). From the loading platform, there is a small stairway (which again can have a door placed in front of) that leads to the water flow and ingredient storage chests which can be used if maintenance is needed (accidental loading of an ingredient into the wrong place). [1.4.7] Survival Warehouse Land Structure Map This is the second version of my warehouse structure I built for a survival multiplayer server. It was designed to house a large amount of items in the most compact way while still retaining usability, design aesthetics, low resource use, visibility and MP protection capabilities.This warehouse holds 100 double chests (200 small chests). Each double chest it meant to be used for either 1 item or a group of related items. The item frame on each chest will display either the item it contains or an item representing the group of items it contains (i.e redstone dust for redstone and redstone related items like torches and pistons).This warehouse will not hold every item in the game if you choose to have a single double chest per item. I tried to design it originally with that in mind but then I would have needed roughly 200+ more double chests and so it becomes too large to look good in my opinion. If you wish to go that route, you can build 2 warehouses next to each other like I had done on the server, one for blocks and one for items.I put in 3 skylights to help light up the interior during the day time. For night time, I put in 4 redstone lamps in the ceiling. I found that this didn't produce enough light for me (on max brightness) so I chose to add a lamp in between each chest. I do not like using glowstone as I find it to look ugly and not something you'd see in a proper building in real life. I like to build in the default texture pack (in this one I am using a higher res default texturepack) so everyone sees it the build like I do. Therefore, replacing the texture was out of the question.I chose to put the chests 1 block inward from the wall to allow access to the back for placement of signs. In MP, most protection plugins use signs to prevent access to chests from un authorized players. Land Structure, Survival, Smp, Chest, Brick, Multiplayer, Warehouse, Deadbolt

[1.6.2] Dungeon Grinder Land Structure Map A dungeon grinder design I came up with for my survival world to farm skeletons. Should work just fine for zombies as well. Uses a simple water trap to push the mob into a lava blade. Under the lava are hoppers to instantly collect the drop. I tested it for a while and didn't see a single item get destroyed in the lava. You can modify the hoppers to either extend or shorten the collection point.I was originally just gonna use my spider dungeon grinder (you can see it here) but I found that it just wasn't quick enough for skeletons. The skeletons took to long to die from the cactus and then most of the loot would fly outward right into the cactus and get destroyed. So I tried to modify the spider dungeon to make the water push the skeletons a little lower before hitting the cactus to prevent the loot from being destroyed but found that I just couldn't make it work so I decided to try and lava blade instead. I'd always wanted to make one but could get one to work well. But I found a nice simple Youtube video here on how to make one and then modified it to work in the grinder.Additional NotesTexture pack: Pixel Perfection Land Structure, Dungeon, Farm, Mob, Hopper, Grinder

3x3 Flush Piston Door Piston Map This is not just your ordinary 3x3 door... This... Is a FLUSH 3x3 door.So... Yeah basically it means that it can be built flat against a wall, without having to have a casing poking out the front. I know that there are other designs that are much better out there, but i'm still proud of this. It's definitely not the most space-efficient design, but the bottom wiring i feel is very compact. It has a dimensions of 6 x 10 x 12, giving a total volume of 720 blocks.I have included below a world save and MC edit schematic for the door. In the world save I have included 3 versions, one that has the wiring covered-ish to show what it could look like normally, and two versions with the wiring exposed, in both orientations so you can see how it works.In the world save I have colour-coded the redstone so it is easier to see how it works.Black: Piston input circuitsGreen: Closing circuitRed: Block storage controlBlue: First block retractionOrange: Double extender controlYellow: Final movement circuitPurple: Top piston circuitsMagenta: Main Input circuitsYou can wire the inputs any way you want, but you have to remember that the top circuit needs to be inverted, as it is closed by default.Please download the world and give it a try, and remember to comment, diamond, and subscribe! Custom Enchantment Table Redstone Device Map This is an enchantment table that, through the use of 5 buttons can be customised so that any number of bookshelves surround it. Each button simple toggles the number of tables that it specifies, so the first button toggles one table, the second 2, ect. Using the first four buttons it is possible to get any number of shelves, from 0 - 15, e.g to get 11 you would press the first, second and fourth buttons (1 + 2 + 8). The final button is just a convenience, and toggles all 15 tables.It is a very simple concept, and just involves the 5 buttons going into separate mono-stable circuits, turning the button press into a one-tick pulse. Each pulse then links to the corresponding pistons, which will toggle whether the shelf is up or down. The final button just links to all 15 tables.In this update I have compacted the wiring very significantly, going from a size of around 1700 blocks, to just a 14 x 9 x 8 volume, giving a total of 1008 blocks, even though it looks like it should be smaller, there needs to be one more air space above where the redstone ends for the pistons to extend in the mono-stable circuits. It now works in 1.5, and should work regardless of location or orientation.Please remember to Diamond, Favorite, Subscribe and Download as this is my first upload to Planet Minecraft, so everything is appreciated!Additional NotesThe server I built this on is called kingcraft, it is a vanilla pvp UK server, but all people are welcome, and can be found at 109.169.12.63, or you can check out the PMC page at www.planetminecraft.com/server/the-real-kingcraft/ Wood, Redstone, Redstone Device, Custom, Button, Sandstone, Piston, Mob

Vous pouvez télécharger cette map gratuitement !MustardSheepEnglishHello everyone !What is it ? : This is my new 2-Way Repeater, it allows you to transmit a redstone signal bi-directionally.How it works ? : When the Input A is on, the signal is sent to B and at the same time, Input B is locked (to avoid infinite loops and so on). This device uses repeater lock technology (available in snapshot 12w42a).Features : - Small and symetric (only 4x3x1)- Easy to build- 0.1s delay (minimum)- Repeater lock technology- No torch (means no burnout)- Transmits all kinds of signals (high frequency clock, direct current and so on)FrançaisBonjour tout le monde !Qu'est-ce que c'est ? : Ceci est un système permettant de transmettre un signal redstone de manière bi-directionnelle.Comment ça marche ? : Quand l'entrée A est à 1, le signal est transmis à B en même temps que l'entrée B est bloquée (pour éviter les boucles infinies). Ce système utilise la technologie des repeaters bloquants (disponible depuis la snapshot 12w42a).Caractéristiques :- Petit, compact et symétrique (4x3x1)- Facile à construire- Délai de 0.1s (minimum)- Utilise les repeaters bloquants- Pas de torches (donc pas de burnout)- Transmet tout type de signal (horloge haute fréquence, courant continu, impulsion etc)Additional Notes*** Texture Pack ***oCd Texture PackTested in 1.5.1 Map, Redstone, Download, Redstone Device, Small, Device, Fast, Sheep

Supermarine Spitfire - WWII Fighter Plane Air Structure Map Get Ready for Duty Pilots!The Supermarine Spitfire is one of the most iconic fighter planes from WWII till this day. Used by the RAF and other Allied forces from 1938 to the mid-1950's, and with over 20,000 built, this powerful beauty of a plane served to be very successful during the Second World War. Speed, firepower and the element of surprise made the Spitfire able to intercept their enemies quick and easily, whether it be protecting and escorting friendly bombers, or skirmishing in the front line. There were many variants of the Spitfire, but the one I have focused on and built is the Mk XIV.Maximum Speed: 448mph/717km/hArmament: 20mm Hispano Cannons x2, 7.7 Browning Machine Guns x4Engine: Rolls Royce Griffon 65 - V12 SuperchargedRate of Climb: 18.5m/sBuild Cost: Approx. £12,000-£13,000Unleash all fury in the skies with this mad machine and make the RAF proud! Compact Hopper Timer (R005) Redstone Device Map Hello everybody, this is Schemetrical, and what I have here is a compact hopper timer. I first got this idea watching Etho's video about his hopper timer. Then, I realized that it was more of a clock, that outputs a signal every once a while. So, I took his design, added and removed a few elements, and made this little guy here. It serves as a real timer, outputs a signal once after the set amount of delay, then stops completely.Take the time you need in seconds, divide by 0.8, and insert that much amount of stackable blocks.This is very useful for timing circuits that need a massive amount of delay, such as potion brewers, stack smelting, and all that crazy time consuming stuff. Question me: ask.fm/SchemetricalMy channel: bit.ly/SchemetI tweet @SchemetricalMail to Schemetrical@gmail.comCredits:Music by Kevin McLeodTexture Pack by ocddisco, modified for extra simplicity, not available as download.Inspired by Etho and his hopper timer! Minecraft, Redstone, Tutorial, Redstone Device, Timer, Customizable, Compact, Delay
